author: Christopher Hoyle
body:
F.I.T. still unpaid as at 05.04.2026, reading submitted 21.02.2026 and acknowledged by Scottish Power.
Here's the e-mail correspondence :

Hello Simo,

It's Chris Hoyle (again).
I've got 2 reference numbers from you : Ref:1896491 and Ref:1895449

Here's a reminder of those e-mails from me that you've kicked into the long grass :


I submitted my FIT meter readings to you on 21 February 2026 and yet I have not been paid
the money due to me. It's over 5 weeks which is totally unacceptable. I've been doing this for
15 years and normally I have to wait 2 weeks, even that is far too long when you consider
that I pay my direct debit bang on time on the day.

Please pay me immediately as this debt is well overdue. In future, I expect to be paid on
the day that I submit the reading and not be held to ransom.

If this matter is not resolved in a satisfactory way, I will switch to a supplier that
has respect for its customers rather than on one that seriously abuses its position to
the detriment of its customers.

Thanks, Chris Hoyle.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello Simo,

The last payment made to me on my account was in 2025.
From my Santander bank statement : 04/12/2025 BANK GIRO CREDIT REF SP LTD, MCGN042964761 £179.40
I invoice and you pay me every 3 months, you first paid me in August 2011, same bank account.
I can confirm that my bank account ends in 7684, sort code 09-01-26.

Thanks, Chris Hoyle.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Simo, I've done everything that I'm supposed to do, you've got all the information that you need
and yet you still think it's acceptable to withhold the money due to me for no legitimate reason.
Please try to justify your rude and offensive behaviour. It's not the amount of money - it's the
principle.

You know what you need to do to stop me taking this further.
If you've got some massive problem here then let me know what it is, keep me informed.
Neglect and abdication will not solve the issue.

Chris Hoyle.
On Friday, 27 March 2026 at 10:50:35 GMT, Chris Hoyle  wrote:


Hello Simo,

Is there another hold up? I gave you all the details that you already had so please pay me. This is long overdue.

Thanks, Chris Hoyle.
On Wednesday, 25 March 2026 at 12:53:17 GMT, Chris Hoyle wrote:


Hello Simo,

The last payment made on my account was in 2025.
From my Santander bank statement : 04/12/2025 BANK GIRO CREDIT REF SP LTD, MCGN042964761 £179.40
I invoice and you pay me every 3 months, you first paid me in August 2011, same bank account.
I can confirm that my bank account ends in 7684, sort code 09-01-26.

Thanks, Chris Hoyle.
On Wednesday, 25 March 2026 at 11:23:25 GMT,  wrote:


Good day Mr Hoyle,



I hope this email finds you well.



The feedback I have received from our Finance Team indicates that the last payment made on your account was in 2024. To ensure we have the correct information on file, could you please confirm whether you are still using the account ending 7684, with sort code 09-01-?



Once we have your confirmation, we can proceed with the necessary checks and updates.



Kind regards,
Simo
FiT Team
ScottishPower
insight: Customer's Feed-in Tariff payment has been delayed over 5 weeks without resolution despite multiple follow-ups and provision of required information, causing significant frustration with the utility's handling of the complaint.

author: Victoria
body:
I have been with Scottish Power for over a decade and I can't fault them. Their staff in their call centres are very friendly and very knowledgeable. They don't push products onto you - if you say no they accept it. 
But usually I can do everything I need online. Their app is great and allows you to manage your account, sign up for new things like the power saver and rewards and control your direct debit. It shows you your usage and I love that you can compare it to previous years. If you do need to speak to someone there is an online chat feature or the phone number. 
I love the power saver initiative where I can select 8 hours a week when I receive half price electricity.
insight: Long-term customer highly satisfied with ScottishPower's app functionality, knowledgeable staff, and innovative power-saving initiatives.

Found the service excellent.

My call was answered in less than 5 minutes baring in mind it was good Friday.

The operator dealt with my issue very quickly and made the nessesary adjustments to my account.
I was very pleased with the efficiency and speed of the whole experience.

author: Mr John Davies

Being on a prepayment meter, we don’t have any unexpected bills and we can just top up our account in large or small amounts from anywhere and at any time. 
This suits us at the moment and we would probably only change to a traditional account if we were to buy an electric car.
insight: Customer appreciates the flexibility and control of prepayment metering, finding it suits their current financial situation and usage patterns.

body: The lad I spoke to was really helpful and said my query was sorted. But so did the last 2 female agents. I asked for email confirmation which all 3 said they would send. Still waiting 🤣 but overall they're all lovely to chat with and seem very helpful. Maybe they just don't like sending emails
insight: Staff are polite and helpful but repeatedly fail to follow through on promised email confirmations, creating a pattern of broken commitments despite the 5-star rating.

author: Steffan Harris
body: I’ll give 4 stars! But  as I moved to Scottish power from a previous provider and still have there smart meter display. Which does not pick up the gas meter any more and I have to manual read the gas meter .. I have been told they can’t send me a new devise .. I’ve had spinal surgery and have rods in my back .. having to get on the floor in the cupboard to read the Meter causes me a lot of pain and discomfort..
insight: User unable to receive replacement smart meter display that reads gas meter, causing physical hardship due to accessibility issues when manually reading the meter.

author: Norman Harvey
body: Over the last 12 months I have been very happy with Scottish Power. The smart metre works well in conjunction with the Scottish Power App. All easily accessible and checking daily consumption + changing Direct Debit amounts and requesting refunds All works really well and hope it continues.
insight: Customer is satisfied with the smart meter functionality and app's ease of use for monitoring consumption and managing billing.

author: Harry Colley
body: App worked fine for ages. About 2 weeks ago it would not let me log in. Everytime I need to top up I have to uninstall the app and the reinstall the app. It works for that log on and then I'm back to square 1.
insight: User experiences repeated login failures requiring app reinstallation each time they attempt to top up their account.

author: PBM Mason
body: can't get on to top up, very difficult to sign in can't use biometric anymore,keeps bouncing bk when reset password takes ages then when you go on again doesn't recognise PW again so all start again nightmare
insight: User is unable to access their account due to repeated authentication failures with biometric and password reset functionality.

author: Dorrie
body:
The graphs clearly show my usage over the 3 years is very similar each year. 
Surprised me, to be honest.
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to visualize long-term energy consumption patterns clearly.

author: batten
body:
Im poor on the internet (85!) but got there
However  app seems defunct not showing true readings
Particularly as  my late wife was on permanent oxygen  via concentrator and I wish to know deceease in usage since December Monthly update imperatve
insight: The app is not displaying accurate meter readings, preventing the user from monitoring energy usage changes following a significant life event.

author: Peter Smith
body: Staff were very helpful
insight: User praised the helpfulness of the staff support team.

body: It’s the Scottish Power professional attitude and appearance that impresses me the most.
insight: User praises Scottish Power's professional attitude and appearance of their staff or service representatives.

body: They came back to me emailing quickly and helpful for me as a landlord.
insight: Customer praised ScottishPower's quick and helpful email response regarding landlord account inquiries.

author: Zelda Mozez
body: Good customer service
insight: User praises the quality of customer service provided by ScottishPower.

author: Jatinder Meyon
body: all good
insight: User is satisfied with the app overall, though the 4-star rating suggests minor room for improvement.

author: Anita anameje
body: App is the worst! I downloaded app less than a month ago and i have uninstall and installed three times already because it's not letting me login
insight: User is unable to log in to the app and has reinstalled it multiple times without resolving the authentication issue.

author: John
body: overal good but at peak times almost impossible to log on
insight: User is generally satisfied with the app but experiences login difficulties during peak usage times.

author: Mick
body:
Whilst it's notoriously difficult to speak a human these days, the experience and support was excellent once I did get through to someone (Jen, I believe). 
Unfortunately, I have to call them back to request a refund (when it eventually shows on the system) as a glitch in the app ended with 2 payments going instead of 1.  So, I'm out of pocket this month.  Surely in this day and age there could be something in place to ensure hard working people can get their money back as quick as these organisations are able to take it. 🤔
insight: App billing glitch caused duplicate payment, and while customer service was helpful, the refund process is slow and leaves customers out of pocket.

author: Richard Clarke
body: It was time to change my fixed tariff. Easy to do through the app & quick to actually do the action needed. Like always it sometimes difficult to actually know exactly what you are buying into fully. Feel I've made the right decision, but can never be 100% sure. Overall though pleased with the transaction.
insight: User successfully changed their fixed tariff through the app but expresses uncertainty about fully understanding the tariff terms and conditions.

author: Susan Cade
body:
Scottish Power have been helpful with my circumstances but I am disappointed with changing to prepayment without being able to see when I need to put money in. 
I asked for details of where I lost so much money not keeping up with payments.
insight: User appreciates customer service support but is frustrated by lack of visibility into prepayment meter balance and unclear communication about payment arrears.

author: MR C COLLINS
body:
i am not at all happy with Scottish Power due to my gas/electric meters going in reverse i.e  less than last readings for March. you must be sure that all meters are working correctly 100%. i am 95 yrs and am very wary of companys. yesterday i rang S P and spoke to a
advisor who in he next few days is going to try to help.
cannot remember his.  Christopher Collins 
have been with SCOT POWER SINCE 2011.
insight: Customer suspects meter malfunction showing reversed readings and is cautiously awaiting promised assistance from advisor Christopher Collins.

author: Angela
body: Given the current financial pressures, I am keen to keep track of my energy usage, but I have noticed a few occasions the information for usage on a particular day does not appear accurate, if, for example, I have done a load of washing and expect my electricity usage to be a little higher, that was not reflected on that particular day’s usage but appeared to be shown on the following day.
insight: Energy usage data displays with a one-day delay, causing inaccurate daily consumption tracking despite the user's intent to monitor usage during high-consumption activities.

author: Dennis Ramsell
body:
Had to deal with a bereaved person's account.
Service was good, only problem was it took nearly 45min to complete on the phone.
Could be simpler and quicker.
insight: Customer appreciated helpful support for a sensitive bereaved account matter but found the 45-minute call duration unnecessarily lengthy and process could be streamlined.

author: Muyiwa
body: Good. The gas meter cabinet is rusted and would appreciate it if you could replace it
insight: User is satisfied with the app but requests maintenance on their gas meter cabinet which is showing signs of rust and needs replacement.

author: Elizabeth
body: The agent on the phone was great. However the service up to that point was poor. As a landlord, we are responsible for the bills during empty periods. Our letting agent had passed you our postal address, but this never reached your billing dept which meant no bills were reaching us. When trying to phone the only option i was given was to key in my account number (which i didnt have as i hadnt had a bill). Holding on just produced the same request 3 times before eventually being put through to an actual (helpful) person. I had given up trying to get through on seversl previous occasions
insight: Customer experienced prolonged billing communication failures due to address information not reaching the billing department, compounded by poor phone system navigation that made it difficult to reach a helpful agent.

author: Maria R
body: Easy to use app to keep on top of bills
insight: User appreciates the app's ease of use for monitoring and managing their energy bills.

author: John Quinn
body: The staff member I spoke to was very helpful and knowledgeable couldn't have been better really. The problem as always is the time to get through and holding times.
insight: Staff quality is excellent but long wait times to reach representatives remain a frustrating barrier to service delivery.

author: Jules
body:
I was with Scottish power for years but transferred just before my fixed rate ended with them. Octopus still had fixed rates so they said I could transfer with no penalties as within the timeframe. Scottish Power penalised me anyway and I had to pay £100 as they said I was 2 days out of the penalty timeframe?!?
I will never return to them for this reason as that just shows what type of company they are…..loyal customers get no special rates and then when you leave they penalise you.
insight: Customer was unexpectedly charged a £100 early exit penalty despite believing they were within the penalty-free transfer window, leading to loss of trust in the company.

author: Janice-Peter James
body: I have no problems with Scottish Power as a power supplier but after 6 years our smart meter still isn’t functioning
insight: Customer is satisfied with the energy supply service but frustrated by a non-functional smart meter that has remained unresolved for 6 years.

author: Richard Hedger
body: The agent was helpful and forwarded my request to the correct person; however, they have yet to respond, which seems to be par for the course.
insight: Agent was responsive but the referred team failed to follow up, indicating systemic communication delays.

author: Nicholas Slabbert
body: easy payment system
insight: User appreciates the simplicity and ease of use of the payment functionality in the app.

author: Lee Turnbull
body: The new verification code process via email is a pain in the ass !
insight: User finds the new email-based verification code process inconvenient and cumbersome.

author: Glenn
body: I like the instant readings enabling me to know my exact costs
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to provide real-time energy consumption data and transparent cost tracking.

author: Shona Porteous
body: The app let's me keep on top of my energy usage and the half price weekends are a great help.

author: Julia Oliveira
body: The worst company that exists. They charged me for 4 years of gas and electricity bills for flats that were not mine. Now I’ve discovered everything and I’m taking legal action against them. They put me under a lot of psychological pressure, with calls, letters, and emails demanding payment of bills in the name of “occupier.” They forced me to pay £8,000. I became ill and had extremely serious financial difficulties trying to pay this. Now we’ve discovered that none of these bills were mine, and after the complaint deadline passes, I will be taking them to court. DO NOT USE SCOTTISH POWER — THEY ARE NOT HONEST.
insight: Customer was incorrectly billed for four years for properties they did not occupy and subjected to aggressive collection practices, resulting in financial and health hardship.

author: ANITA H
body: On the whole they provide a good service and prices are usually competitive. Via the website or app, customers have control over their direct debit amount and there are useful tools to keep track of energy usage and compare to previous years. I had some issues last year , but they were sorted out eventually to my satisfaction. Recently they changed the half price electricity weekend deal to give customers more choice of time slots to save. Sounded good on the face of it but I think Scottish Power think customers won’t notice that they’ve reduced it from 10 hours at the weekend to 8 hours . Well I have noticed ! That said it was useful to be able to choose a better time to save. I hope they continue to give us the best prices when energy costs rise in July.
insight: Customer appreciates competitive pricing and app control features but feels misled by reduced savings hours in the restructured weekend electricity deal.

author: Arun
body: Not a good responsibility customer service,  making money only, not a good deal offers tariff telling ext..IMPORTANT very worst customer care agents. Always Rod speaks
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with poor customer service quality, unfavorable tariff offers, and feels the company prioritizes profit over customer care.

author: Sian Evans
body:
Avoid! We’re new to Scottish Power and asked them to set up our gas and electric account. They set up our gas but not electric.

 They then closed our gas account without telling us and opened an electric account. When they re-opened the gas account, they set it up wrong, we haven’t been able to top-up our gas and now being left without hot water or heating as they say they cannot top it up from there side. 

Awful customer service, they do not listen to you. There’s so much background noise, so you cannot hear them. 

They will happily let you go without hot water or heating. 

Moving to British Gas and already had a much better experience.
insight: Multiple account setup errors combined with poor customer service resulted in loss of essential utilities and a switch to a competitor.

author: LINDA DEAVES
body: Scottish power issue of not registering my electric meter on national grid caused me stress anxiety and time and energy when I was caring for both parents with dementia. I rang n rang n went to Venus women's support centre for their help to contact. I ended up 2 years with ombudsman deciding I owed over £2000. I am on benefits........then I had to ring up to get the 4 action points done by them. I finally got a smart meter n I have been paying off the debt for 2 years........terrible experience with them.
insight: Customer experienced severe financial and emotional distress due to unregistered meter, unresolved billing dispute, and poor service recovery over a 4-year period.

(I will still give 5*)

author: Alan
body: got a phone call today about why I have posted a bad review about Scottish power. This the normal route they take ie: about 7 phone calls all about this problem with my wall box charger they sold me .they agreed as i had to wait a year for the ground works to complete by Scottish power networks so charger was only fitted for 2 years .I got so frustrated missing appointments cause the charger did not charge during the night ,as this is for a mobility car. I had to either use it as a dumb charger or it would not work at all so what they said is go to wall box and get a new charger fitted as they agreed it was still under warranty . By this time we are 2 years of complaining. wall box are- if you would believe anybody can top Scottish power for bad customer support even worse .the give generic computer responses about 3 to 4 day later to complaints and instruction on how to fix the rubbish wall box which do not work. So i ripped the wall box off the wall in frustration as i had to use public charging again. Got a  hyper volt charger fitted at my cost which works as advertised now Scottish power are saying because I've lost patience with them and wall box  they are not going to refund me ,they should refund me for a product they sold me not fit for purpose and compensate me for the years of pubic charging and frustration and stress the have caused me. Update they phoned me today with no resolution no refund no way forward and are now claiming the charger is out of warranty -seriously ???.WHAT A PAIR OF GREAT COMPANYS BUYER BEWARE ?not a chance of money back for Scottish power-like their power it only flows one way and have no regard for their customers so now I have to go to citizens advice and my local member of parliament as no ombudsman in place for this complaint. I will post bad reviews forever   about this as i feel I have been scammed by Scottish power.update on Jodie answer they have said they can't and will not help me !????
insight: Customer experienced a year-long dispute over a faulty EV wall charger with poor support from both ScottishPower and Wallbox, ultimately paying out-of-pocket for a replacement and being refused compensation.

author: frequent user
body:
Bills are being issued in the Welsh language - never requested.

We have a type 1 smart meter  which, we think, provides meter readings to Scottish Power, or its agents, every 30 minutes or so. It does not communicate with the household unit so we can’t monitor our power useage - this issue has been flagged for 2 years and once again has been passed to the technical department who have been tasked with contacting us within 7 days of our discussion with customer services.
insight: Customer is frustrated by unsolicited Welsh-language bills and a two-year-old smart meter connectivity issue that remains unresolved despite repeated promises to contact them.

author: Andrew Dunsire
body: If you are considering using Scottish Power for charging your electric vehicle I suggest you read this debacle. On setting up an EV tariff in January 2025 to take advantage of the off peak rate between midnight and 5 in the morning. After a few months the bill I received showed an enormous amount of electricity consumed and was obviously incorrect. Subsequently my bills look incorrect with the proportion of off peak and peak consumption not agreeing to my meter readings in the house. My ScottishPower App is showing consumption in the wrong half hour slots. We are now a year or so on and I just get passed from pillar to post. The information we need to unravel this I am told as it will “overload the system “.I have spent days of my time on this and have no confidence that it will get sorted. I believe I have to reach deadlock before I take the matter to the Small Claims Court and Ofgen.
insight: Customer's EV tariff billing has been incorrect for over a year with misaligned meter readings and app data, but ScottishPower refuses to provide necessary information to resolve the issue, leaving the customer frustrated and considering legal action.

body: Problems with a new smart meter installed in November 2026 after the previous one failed. Since then I receive no information on the app/online account for gas usage. One day they blame the app, the next day I’m assured that all app issues have been resolved. When I request a visit to check that the smart meter is working correctly they tell me that it is and it must be a problem with the app. Four plus months of this nonsense and the merry go round continues!!!

author: Lizzie
body: Scottish Energy is the worst energy company I have ever come across. Just absolute garbage of a company. They harass elderly people for a bill they do t owe and for which they were never their supplier. They send bills for a pay as you go when you move from their company and unable to explain why this isn’t included in your top ups. They send you to another number which never answers calls. Just a horrible company. I hope you get closed down.

author: Karen Palacio

author: Rita Zadi
body:
Speechless. After more than a year of disputes, I’ve ended up with a credit mark and a debt collector involved—yet still no clear explanation, no proper resolution, and no accountability.

My winter reduction payment was received, but it has never appeared on my account. Despite raising this multiple times, nothing has been corrected.

To make matters worse, when I tried to pay the debt collector, the payment failed twice due to “technical issues.” On top of that, the amount requested by Richburn differs from what was shown on my original SP account—lower, but unexplained. This inconsistency raises serious concerns.

I have kept all evidence throughout this process. From my experience, this does not feel like simple error or mismanagement—it feels like a system that allows people to be misled and financially affected without proper oversight.

This situation has caused unnecessary stress and damage to my credit record. I expected professionalism, transparency, and basic accountability—none of which have been delivered.

I strongly urge this to be investigated and taken seriously so others do not go through the same experience.
insight: Customer's payment was not credited to account, debt collector became involved, and repeated attempts to resolve resulted in no accountability or proper explanation over 12+ months.

body: Well treated
insight: User is satisfied with the quality of customer service and treatment received from ScottishPower.

author: Susan Jones
body: Only updated 5 days ago. Had to uninstall and reinstall app due to the annoying "email code" which to get you have to go off the app/website, get the code, back to app and start all over again by which time the code won't work as timespan has run out! Also why, when I want to sign in to app is it taking me to the flaming website! Oh also I've had to update app again today for some reason. Tried again - sorry app now absolutely rubbish! Can't get in at all. Deleting it and will use website
insight: User is unable to access the app due to broken email code authentication process and frequent crashes, forcing them to switch to the website.

author: Mohamed
body: Good customer service
insight: User praises the quality of customer service provided by ScottishPower.

author: Al
body:
From an experience i can say they have the worst customer service ever 
They have a High standing charge and they would send me a bill with a high price out of the sudden then say they have been calculating my bill wrong. They refuse to let me speak to the managers and keep telling me to end the line then call again if i wanted to speak to another agent. Mind you i’m on their priority service.
insight: Customer experiences billing errors and poor customer service quality, including inability to escalate to managers despite being on priority service.

author: Paul Bowskill
body: I discovered today that Scottish Power have been billing me fortnightly for the past 2 months but failed to inform me or provide a reason for this. I regularly submit my meter readings monthly in order to manage my account but to discover this change in their billing was something of an unwelcome surprise, especially as I maintain a credit balance
insight: Customer was not informed of an unexplained change from monthly to fortnightly billing despite maintaining regular meter submissions and a credit balance.

author: Roxanne Crook
body: this app is trash
insight: User expresses strong dissatisfaction with the app but provides no specific details about the underlying problem.

author: IRVINE PATMAN
body: Just wanted to know how i stood with my money for paying  the bill as i could not understand  the bill statement.
insight: User appreciated the app's help in clarifying their bill statement and understanding their account balance.

author: Josephine Amegatcher
body: Straight forward to understand anything you are not sure of
insight: User appreciates the app's clarity and straightforward interface for understanding account information.

author: John Wright
body: The advisor was able to put my readings in for me as the app would not accept the reading because I dont have any change on my old heat meter that was not removed six years ago when new heating was installed.
insight: User's meter reading submission failed in the app due to an outdated heat meter, but the issue was resolved when a customer service advisor manually entered the reading.

author: Jericho Javens
body: can't login just going round and round with emails that don't work the app needs sorting ASAP
insight: User is unable to access their account due to a login loop issue and non-functional email communications from the app.

author: Stephanie Warne

insight: User is frustrated by a delayed feature rollout that was promised over a month ago.

author: Ray Toussaint
body: It was a good service
insight: User had a generally positive experience with the service but lacks specific detail about what worked well.

author: Elizabeth

author: Levi Perry

author: Andrew Sarosi
body: useless. doesn't work. typical Scottish power IT and customer service incompetence

author: Mr Hamilton
body: Costing us a fortune
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with high energy costs charged by ScottishPower.

author: Kerry Gray
body: Had to uninstall the app as got stuck on log in page wont log in, sends an email to change password but link says already used when I haven't! Luckily for some reason my husband was able to still log in on his phone so we were able to top up for now. Not looking forward to calling customer services tbh as takes ages to get through and not normally helpful unfortunately. EDIT 4 days later and ive just had to uninstall the app again as I can't log in, getting annoying now as Im a top up customer.
insight: User is unable to log in due to a faulty password reset mechanism and is frustrated by poor customer service accessibility.

author: Alex Hammond
body: The advisor I spoke to was extremely helpful in guiding me to the pages I needed to complete to get my Solar Power Tarriff arranged.
insight: Customer praised advisor for providing helpful guidance on completing solar power tariff setup.

author: Joy Pinkton
body:
The young man I spoke to was so helpful.   He explained fully how my electricity and gas were billed.   He also gave me an understanding about variable and fixed deals making it easier for me to make a decision.
However, Elderly people, like me, should not have to make these decisions.   There should be one tariff specific for elderly people, especially if they live alone.
insight: Customer praises helpful staff support for billing explanation but requests a dedicated tariff option for elderly people living alone.

author: Michael Brown
body: This must be the first app I've ever had to deal with. first, I got logged out and couldn't log in, and then I finally logged in. it updated and logged me out, and I couldn't log in. it keeps giving me an error message that something is wrong. Try later. I cleared data and catch and started again, and then it said the device was authenticated. I tried to log in again, and it was the same issue over and over. some of the emsil links are giving error messages as either expired or used already.
insight: User is trapped in a repeating authentication loop where the app repeatedly logs them out and refuses to allow login despite multiple troubleshooting attempts.

author: Stephen Mckinlay
body: Helpful , friendly and resolved my query.
insight: User received helpful and friendly customer service that successfully resolved their query.

author: John Lebeau
body: Great so easy to do love it 😀
insight: User finds the app straightforward and enjoyable to use.

author: Nazira Begum
body: Really helpful
insight: User found the app or service helpful and satisfactory.

author: Robert Haywood
body: Very helpful in regards to using cheap energy, also introduced me to other offers.
insight: User appreciates the app's ability to help manage energy costs and discover additional offers.

author: Sandra Brown
body: good
insight: User gives a positive word rating but contradicts it with a 2-star rating, suggesting significant dissatisfaction despite minimal feedback.

author: Mrs Carole Hanmer
body:
The girl who was the wrong department  tried to help me and was deserving of 4*...Im hoping what she told me was correct but believe she thinks it is
On tge whoke its so difficult to get through to anyone to speak to and I couldn't access my account on line..one of my problems...and tge Chat bot thing told me I wasnt me....with my info
insight: User struggled to reach customer service, experienced chatbot authentication issues, and received uncertain assistance from wrong department staff.

author: jonesevans
body: Ridiculous now. Email/password, Email with code sent, come off Scot Power app to email app. Can’t open both at same time to view code to enter code on phone app. Tried copy code (to paste in box), back to Scot Power app, but can’t enter code as it goes back to email/password enter box. Also when open app, tap log in - takes me to website! As someone else it’s easier getting on a banking app than this. This is just an energy account no one else can use it really anywhere else. Try a text message code instead - I’ve used that on apps/website and the notification appears over the app/page I’m viewing.
insight: The two-factor authentication process is severely broken, requiring users to exit the app to view codes and preventing code entry due to repeated redirects to the login screen.

author: Robert Davison
body: Ur colleague's make things easy for my mum,dad and me when phoning and go through it step by step to make sure things are ok 👍 thank u 💙💙 Customer service plus staff.
insight: Customer praises the helpful and patient approach of staff when assisting family members over the phone.

author: SNAFU 001
insight: No review text provided to analyse.

author: Iulian Duma Trunchi
body: I am very happy

author: Barbara Cormack
body: The app is great but the Customer Service is appalling... told off for not submitting readings, then waiting months for the invoice. Why is generating the invoice not automatic? Why does it take so long to generate? When will Scottish Power update my balance?
insight: Customer is frustrated with poor customer service, manual billing processes, and delayed invoice generation and account balance updates.

author: Murial Stuart
body: I can view my account at any time
insight: User appreciates the ability to access their account information conveniently through the app at any time.

author: Andrew
body: The Customer service agent did the best to deal with my problem what was unable to because Scottish Power at the present does not have any Smart meter displays at present to replace the one I have, which is not working correctly
insight: Customer service agent provided good support but was unable to resolve the faulty smart meter display issue due to current unavailability of replacement units.

author: Happy man.
body:
Don't advertise you choose reduced cost hours if you can't actually deliver them.
It says you can choose your own hours.
But I can't get onto that part of the website. It just repeatedly says error retry.
Your customer service also can't  get access on my  behalf.
However, meanwhile, I am getting
Reduced hours at the weekends,thanl you.
insight: User cannot access the reduced cost hours feature due to repeated website errors, and customer service is unable to resolve the issue on their behalf.

author: James Phillips
body: Very helpful and answered call fast
insight: User praised the speed and helpfulness of customer support when they made a call.

author: KJ
body: Keep getting young people from Scottish Power coming to my door trying to convince me to change my energy provider. They don’t introduce themselves so have to ask them who they represent and has asked them to stop coming to th door to no avail. About 5 different visits in the last few months.
insight: Customer is frustrated by repeated unsolicited door-to-door sales visits from ScottishPower representatives who ignore requests to stop calling.

author: Amanda Canning
body: whats happing with the app only had an issue this week with trying to top up my meter sick of trying to log in even though I never log out can anyone help resolve this issue as am stressed to the max 🤬🤬🤬
insight: User is unable to log in to complete a meter top-up payment despite never logging out, causing significant frustration.

author: Tony Idiong
body: there is barely no one at home during the day, yet my electricity bill keeps going up and up
insight: Customer is concerned about unexpectedly high electricity bills despite low daytime usage, suggesting a potential metering or billing accuracy issue.

author: Kathleen Finney
body: never shows you on your app on your phone how mutch your using on gas and electric having to punch number in on meters all the time

author: Anthony Ashworth
body: Twice I’ve put a review on trust pilot and twice Scottish power have commented saying someone will be in touch but not heard a thing !! Simply the worst company I have ever dealt with in my 52 years of life .

author: Stacey
body: You work your sales agents like absolute idiots! Unable to go home until we get you sales yes you don’t pay u til we make 11 sales!! 60 quid a day yet we are out from 8an until 7pm and still not home at 7.40! Actual slave labour you want to be ashamed of yourselfs
insight: Employee criticizes exploitative sales agent working conditions with inadequate pay and excessive hours.

author: Lyn Douglas
body: rubbish it won't load. won't accept my password. too frustrating

author: SentaiGreen
body: App is constantly not allowing logins, I have to uninstall, reinstall, then login and authenticate via email just to top up my gas and leccy. utterly unusable garbage app now. fix it Scottish Power
insight: User experiences repeated login failures requiring app reinstallation, preventing access to top-up functionality.

author: ann “Annie” Bool
body: no problem with this company
insight: User expresses overall satisfaction with ScottishPower's service with no specific complaints.

author: Susan Guest
body: had great problems with trying to get the new power saver spoke to a gentleman from technical team his name was Lee he was so patient and knowledgeable guiding me through the process l can't thank him enough.
insight: User praised exceptional technical support from staff member Lee who helped resolve issues with the power saver feature.

author: Simon Jennings
body: Your callhandlers put tus in a cheap tarrif to charge an EV.  However this is not possible under your company  rules. So it kept defaulting to  a standard tarrif and was expensive.  I have raised a complaint and I belive this has been sorted  (thank you Karen) but time will tell.  I also had to block your number due to constant calls  and eventually your company  got around this by using another telephone number.
insight: Customer was placed on an unsuitable EV tariff that kept reverting to standard rates, resulting in high charges, and experienced persistent unwanted contact from the company despite blocking their number.

author: Ian B
body: Due to my lack of computer knowledge it was easy to understand and use what I was looking for.
insight: User appreciates the app's intuitive design and accessibility for those with limited technical expertise.

author: Molly Reilly
body: I am looking to talk to someone properly I have recently moved house and keep getting debt collection letters at my new door from the old tenants this is my first time moving out alone and I payed for the first bill of letters that came in not knowing it wasn’t my debt I’m looking for my money back and the letters to stop as I am now with British Gas who have been much better at communicating and customer service all round. I have been phoning up Scottish power for 3 days now emailing and now this is my second review, can someone please contact me that will actually help out as the people on the customer service team are rude, unhelpful, laughing at me in distress, hung up on me and shouting at me down the phone 3 different women I spoke all 3 were shockingly rude and unhelpful.
insight: Customer paid for previous tenant's debt in error and is seeking refund and resolution, but has experienced multiple instances of rude, dismissive, and unhelpful customer service across three separate support interactions.

author: Allan Webb
body: App usually works OK, but after updating on 24/03/2026 I can't log in. I continually get "Oops something went wrong. Please try again later". Username, password and authentication code are all OK. Update: eventually logged in, after clearing cache, re-installing app and endlessly going round in circles with authentication codes and error messages. Update 31/03/2026: another app update, and back to square one with logging in again 😟
insight: User experiences recurring authentication failures after app updates that require workarounds and persist across multiple versions.

author: Jennifer Lowles

author: Isobel campbell
body:
I think quite possibly the worst services I have received in a long time. 

I am a young first time home owner, and as everybody knows how stressful moving and everything with that is, Scottish Power did not help this at all. 

They first and foremost cut our supply for over 24 hours with no help and no mention of sending out an engineer. 

 I called 9 times, and 3 of them times I was meant to receive a call back which I did not. 

Now I have figured it out my own enquiry on my own. After days of dealing with this company. 

I never leave negative reviews, however how the staff has dealt with my service and any issues I have had has been ridiculous and quite frankly disgusting.
insight: Customer experienced a 24+ hour supply outage with inadequate support, unmet callback promises, and poor staff handling during a stressful house move.

author: Timothy Cox
body: The staff are helpful nothings is to much trouble also I like the half price electric at weekends and I can manage my account on with ease

author: Harry Powers
body: The company and customer service are terrible.
insight: User is highly dissatisfied with both the company and its customer service quality.

author: Khaled Miah
body: TOTAL SHAMBLES. A year of constant issues and a useless app that never works. Scottish Power is the most unreliable supplier in the UK. Don't even think about replying with your "sorry for the experience" script—save your breath. You’ve had a year to fix this and failed. I’m moving back to EDF energy today. Do not contact me ever again... I should have never left EDF Energy...
insight: Customer experienced persistent app failures and poor support over a year-long period, leading them to switch providers.

author: brian hunter

author: D P
body:
The app is excellent for day to day use, providing accessible information, as and when needed.
However, it is not good at answering queries, particularly about tariffs, options, providing advice. However, they do respond through text, with further information and advice, when queries are raised.
insight: App excels at providing account information but lacks self-service query resolution, requiring users to contact support via text for tariff and advice questions.

author: Miss Denton
body: I've just switched to prepayment meter, only because once it was explained to me properly and how much money I could save that, i thought it would be better for me. But also being in control of what I spend or don't spend. I would like to mention the service I received from the door salesman, he was professional,  polite and knowledgeable.  And went the extra mile and helped me set up the app.
insight: Customer appreciates the professional service from the salesman and app setup assistance, choosing prepayment meter for cost savings and spending control.

author: Christine Rothery
body: excellent service
insight: User is satisfied with the overall service quality provided by ScottishPower.

author: Joanne
body: The advisor on the phone was fine and couldn't really help as the issue is my smart meter is not connecting and there is no way to tell what my monthly bills should be as I dont have access to the outside meter as SP locked the door on the outside box so now could end up in debt and struggle to pay this as there are no engineer appointments for 6MONTHS !! Outrageous .Thanks for your reply , as the keys are cheap and widely available this is good as you can now send these to customers who need them ?
insight: Customer is unable to access their smart meter due to a locked external box and faces a 6-month wait for engineer repairs, leaving them unable to monitor consumption and at risk of debt.

author: Mr Carl Bowler
body: Poor app lack of features working and missing ev optimizer only gives 80% or what it actually used so your not actually getting the rate that you think you will be when you work out the extra unaccounted power your meter recorded    poor customer service on the chat they don’t even know what products they offer or seemingly how they work. overall very disappointed I will be changing supplier

author: John Pye
body: I would not go so far as to say the company was great. The chap I spoke to this morning was very good , trying to get someone to talk to was not so easy.
insight: Customer service representative was helpful but accessibility to reach someone was difficult.

author: Stephen Leggate
body: I have both gas and electric from Scottish Power. I have a smart meter for gas which works perfectly. However, after a failed attempt to set up a smart meter for electricity 5 years ago I am no further forward getting one installed. Despite being bombarded on a daily basis by adverts on TV urging everyone to get a smart meter, and making frequent requests,Scottish Power have made no attempt to supply one. In fact, to add insult to injury, I recently received an email congratulating me on getting one. Scottish Power have not been near my house and I don't have a smart meter. Something is far wrong with the system. Scottish Power need to do better.
insight: Customer has been unable to obtain an electricity smart meter installation for 5 years despite repeated requests and misleading communications from Scottish Power.

author: Wieslawa pankito
body: I am very happy with Scottish Power app
insight: User expresses overall satisfaction with the ScottishPower app despite giving a 4-star rather than 5-star rating.

author: Dani Barnes

insight: Customer is frustrated with persistent payment plan issues and aggressive provider switching solicitation despite setting up an agreed payment arrangement.

author: Norman Johnston

author: melvyn henderson

author: Brandon McConnell
body: Terrible customer service over a year I’ve chased them up about a debt they put on my credit file when calling up there was no record of this. 1 year in and I’ve been calling weekly emailing daily and still at the same stage. Constant promises that you will hear back that day and not once over a minimum of 10 promises has this happened. Now having to work with the energy ombudsman and today started proceedings with the courts. The stress and hassle this has caused me for over a year and as mentioned nothing changes. I’m now left with no option but to cost myself money to take this to a court and solicitor.
insight: Customer has spent over a year attempting to resolve an incorrectly reported debt with no meaningful progress despite repeated contact attempts and broken promises from the company.

author: JOSEPH MAWERE Opoku

author: Owen
body:
Ive had The same issue since December its now 30th March, all I needed to do was change direct debit dates, this would normally be straightforward of a job for any company except Scottish power.
Ive been on the phone a total of 8 different times and each phone call lasting over an hour to do this. I wanted to change from the 6th of each month to the 21st of each month due to changing jobs and the pay dates were different.
I thought this was sorted after the first 2 conversations but they went on to take payments on the 14th and then the 16th then after more conversations and more people telling me they have 100% sorted it they just didnt take payment at all. The amount of stress this has caused me has 100% made me realise I will never refer anyone to Scottish power, you could pardon a mistake once of twice due to the language barrier that was in hand but this much hassle for a simple objective is bone idle and lack of care from customer services. 
Then the last lady I spoke to she was worth her weight in gold, finally someone at Scottish power who cared, she didn't only manage to do the "impossible" of changing a direct debit date but she also managed to find a fault with my gas meter she jumped through all the hoops and processes to sort it out for me and got it sorted and scheduled a follow-up call to see if 1, the payment come out on the chosen date and 2, if the gas meter is up and running still. If Scottish power had more of that lady working with them they would have a long list of happy customers and achieve great things, instead ive had to go through multiple people who didn't care about me as a customer and had absolutely no enthusiasm towards what job they were clocked in to do and get paid for.
insight: A 4-month struggle to change direct debit dates due to repeated errors and poor customer service, though one exceptional staff member ultimately resolved the issue and identified a gas meter fault.

author: Beth McCrossan
body: can't even log in, why does everything have to be an app that doesn't work
insight: User is frustrated by inability to log in and perceives the app as fundamentally unreliable.

author: Stephen Clarke
body: Keeping my gass and electric bills in one place .easier for me
insight: User appreciates the convenience of managing both gas and electricity bills through a single app interface.

author: Gman Can
body:
If I could give less than 1 star then I would.

They are breathtakingly incompetent.

In the past 3 years I have spent a total of more than 6 hours on the phone trying to resolve an issue which is entirely caused by Scottish Power. The call centre staff are generally very pleasant and usually helpful. I get assured repeatedly that they understand the issue and they say that it is all written in the file for the next person to see but mostly that does not happen. I get further calls but the caller cannot see that I have been told the issue is resolved (It has not been). The cycle of confusion continues....just rinse and repeat, as it were. The final straw came today when I was contacted by solicitors chasing a debt but they had been instructed the same day that Scottish Power last assured me that there was no problem.

The Scottish Power account is in the name of my elderly sister who has dementia and Alzheimers but lives alone. She is on the S.P. VUlnerable Person list (but keeps getting removed from that). I have authorisation to deal with S.P. and hold a power of attorney. The customer has a direct debit arrangement in place and I have been repeatedly told by S.P. on the phone that they are happy and the account details are correct BUT when they issue bills, the bills say on them that the agreed amount will be taken on a particular date. The invoices say clearly "you do not need to do anything further". S.P. then fails to take the direct debit (despite assurances that they will) and so the debt builds up. I have repeatedly been told that the problem is solved. On 23rd March £588 was paid on a debit card at the request of S.P. who also agreed a new monthly direct debit amount to clear the outstanding debt....at an amount higher than what they were suggesting. The customer does not dispute the bills and is quite willing to pay by the direct debit that S.P. holds.....however, S.P. just fails to take the money owing and it is not the fault of the customer or her bank. So I believed that the matter was resolved by S.P. on 23rd of March when I spoke several times to a woman called Ndombe at their call centre in South Africa who totally assured me thaT Scottish Power was now happy and it had been all their fault. Later that  same day S.P. instructed Flint Bishop Solicitors to persue the customer despite telling me that the issue was resolved and had been the fault of Scottish Power. If this matter continues, I will not rest until it has been aired in the press and on TV consumer shows.
insight: Customer experienced years of unresolved billing and direct debit failures compounded by poor internal communication across call centre staff, resulting in debt collection action despite repeated assurances of resolution.

author: Valerie Sturgess
body: very good
insight: User is satisfied with the overall quality of the app or service.

author: Joan Baxter

She helped me pay for my gas and electric on line, as sometimes I am not quick enough to type in the details or the machine does not understand what I am saying. The line went dead at one point, she very kindly called back, so we could finish the transaction. At no time did I say I was leaving.
insight: Customer praised helpful support staff who assisted with online payment and reconnected after a dropped call.

author: Hafeez Rehman
body:
Switched my Dual account to Scottish power after asking the support team if my vehicle is eligible for anytime charge, which I was confirmed. Then checked on Scottish power Eligiblity checker multiple times and answer was eligible. So went ahead and switched accounts one by one, switching electric was another storey as was told, they already have an active Scottish power Electric account on my address, strange enough as I have been with Octopus since last 6 years. After about 5 calls that was sorted.

Now I have been told Scottish can't help me as they are unable to connect my vehicle on anytime change rate and been advised to take 12-5am saver Charging offer, in this process I have wasted a week and x 3 money to Scottish on charging in comparison to what I was paying to Octopus. So feeling clearly been let down by dishonest people. 

Based on my experience I will advise, Please double check that you are getting what you have been promised if you are switching to Scottish Power.
Thanks
insight: Customer was promised EV charging eligibility but ScottishPower could not deliver on the service after switching, resulting in wasted time and higher costs compared to their previous provider.

author: Abysmal Abysmal
body: Six months to replace or repair a smart meter display/communication unit is very poor.

author: A M
body:
This app has caused a lot of trouble for our family. Using the same email address and the same home address, it created two separate accounts for me (it might have been due to something I did, but I’m not sure what went wrong).

Another issue is that my wife wanted to access our account on her phone, but she couldn’t. It seems she would need to log in using my email on her device, which is very inconvenient. We haven’t even tried further because the process is too complicated.

It has been almost a month since we opened the account, and my account is still not functioning properly.
insight: Account creation and access issues have left the user unable to properly use the app after nearly a month, with duplicate accounts and inability for multiple users to access the same account.

author: Hayley Spicer

author: sunnie Odwyer
body: From the start of me switching over I've had nothing but issues. I paid my £124 each month, i had £298 in my balance account, so I asked for a refund if your not using my account and then just decided to take £250 i asked why have you taken this, because I haven't give a meter reading, so I gzve meter readings and got £60 back, I will never recommend Scottish power to anyone and they still haven't give me a good reason why I apparently used all this gas and electric
insight: Customer experienced unexplained charges and unauthorized account deductions without clear explanation from ScottishPower despite providing meter readings.

author: Michael Gendon
body:
I would give zero if possible but it’s not.

I received an email from Scottish Power regarding smart meters.

They obviously have not heard of the lie of omission. They say about dispelling myths. What they do not say is that smart meters enables them to remotely cut you off without any justification.

How can I trust a company like that when they have increased my bill whilst they recognise that they owe me even more than last year and refuse to refund.

Do not trust them. The unusually high amount of five stars reviews indicate so called AI bots paid for by Scottish Power.

Added as a reply to Scottish Power. The problem is your reply is an actual lie. I have to go through hoops and argue to get any refunds and that’s after waiting 30 minutes to an hour for an answer of a phone call. And then wait a month for a repayment.

As for cutting people off no one believes that you have not cut anyone off who has switched to electronic meter readers. As there has been a high level of complaints regarding your company and cutting people off.
insight: Customer distrusts ScottishPower due to concerns about remote disconnection capabilities via smart meters, unresolved billing disputes, and poor refund processes requiring excessive contact efforts.

author: Mr Nigel Dixon
body:
The advisor I eventually got to talk to was very helpful and professional. I wanted to find out my rates and thought it would be on my account, NO!
I then went for frustrating chat with a bot###
I asked to be out through to a human, all agents are busy! Very frustrating to round in circles. Eventually I phoned and got holed of the agent and blood pressure went down.
insight: User experienced frustrating delays with chatbot and long wait times for human agents, though eventual phone support resolved the issue satisfactorily.

As I'm disabled, I rely on this to be able to top up my pre pay meters, so my gas and electricity doesn't get shut off. I reached out to the support team and they just said they're aware of customers having issues.
insight: User is unable to access the app due to a login loop issue, preventing essential top-ups for pre-pay meters, and support has provided no meaningful resolution.

Why do you keep advising me to have a Smart Meter when the Engineer has been to assess the situation and advised me that it is not possible to fit one!! Second review of the day. As an 80 year old women, I found the previous version easy. This updated version is overly complicated. I only want to put my metre readings in and can’t get to the correct page.

Meter Readings

The next meterreading is due and I haven’t changed my opinion. This new version is a complete mystery to me. How do you put your meter readings in? I’ve pressed every button but no good.
I’m afraid, I’m going to have to change suppliers. I can’t put up with this every three or four months. It was so easy before. Now it’s  just confusion. 
Geraldine Bruton Customer
insight: Elderly customer finds the redesigned app overly complicated and cannot easily submit meter readings, threatening to switch suppliers.
