author: steve hale
body: never works when you need it the most !
insight: User experiences app failures during critical moments when they need to access their energy account.

author: Tara Godin
body:
Will my nightmare with Scottish power ever end?! 
Spent hours on the phone settling a bill for months, which was wrong as it was based on estimates rather than current usage. On settling the bill, I was assured by staff that my account had been closed. 
Today, I received a text message that a bill for £0.83 will be with me shortly. I absolutely can not believe this. I’ve lost so much energy going through costumer service team. Beyond exasperated.
insight: Customer spent hours resolving an incorrectly estimated bill and was told their account was closed, only to receive another unexpected charge shortly after.

author: Karen Hallam
body:
Honestly this company! 🤬
Received a call on Friday 27/3, a week after my supply was swapped to Eon, to say they were sorry that I was due to leave them… erm… I’d already left and they didn’t know?! Then I told her I’d previously made a complaint, which she had no knowledge of, proceeding to ask me many times whether it was my meter or the smart meter that was faulty as they’re split into 2 parts - something that she should have been able to see from the engineer’s report from the week before. I don’t know which cowing bit of it is faulty because I’m not a bloody engineer!!! 🤬
You’ll get my settlement of £24.58 for my gas bill by April 7th and that will be me done with you!
Danial already agreed to zero my electricity account when my meter is fixed by Eon, so I’ll owe you nothing once the gas is paid. 
Also, I didn’t agree to close the complaint, it was closed by SP because I was switching supplier. 

I’m actually very happy to be with Eon now, as their Next Smart Saver Fixed 12m tariff gives me 3 hours of half price electricity every day, and a further 19 hours every day at a vastly reduced price!! HUGE SAVINGS!!
insight: Customer experienced poor service during exit process, including lack of knowledge of previous complaints and unclear communication about meter faults, leading to satisfaction with switching to competitor.

author: Miss Susan Meah
body: I am happy with SP and will remain with them, I liked the half price weekend usage.
insight: Customer is satisfied with ScottishPower and appreciates their half-price weekend usage tariff offering.

author: Tracie Tasker
body: i cant log in i cant get gas or electric because i don't have the bar codes now absolutely useless, just one big loop i cant top up
insight: User is unable to top up their energy account due to missing barcodes and being trapped in a confusing app loop, preventing access to gas and electricity services.

author: J Tay
body:
Disgusting company thats got everything wrong from the moment I switched.
Upon switching the app failed to let me top up or see my usage or balance, they added credit to my account but didn't state I'd need to pay the electric back, not the gas which is weird.
Now 2 months later I have been logged out the app, the password that works on the website doesn't work. I've uninstall it, reinstalled it. Done forgot password, done password reset, done temporary password and been in the online support chat for an hour and 20 to be told try logging back in 24 hours and goto the shop to top up.
So now with a smart meter while it's snowing i have to manually get top ups and enter them into my meters because the support staff ignored my request to have £10 added to both my electric and gas amd switching to on supply and not prepayment takes around 3 days & not advised to do. Great help!

Do not join this useless company, if their app stops working there is no telephone support at the weekend, omar from whatever 3rd world country who types slow because he only has 2 working fingers is no use & neither was the other support staff member. They take around 10 minutes to answer a question ffs..

You cannot see your balance on the website, that's pretty crap! 

You cannot top up on the website.

You cannot view your usage on the website.

I've put in my request to go back to Utilita tonight, I had no issues with them for the last 4 years.
insight: Multiple app and website failures combined with poor customer support have left the user unable to manage their prepayment account, forcing them to return to their previous supplier.

author: NICOLA HUDSON
body: Advisor was polite and helpful. Payments offered were high which wasn't helpful, no prepaid meter option even though this was given as an option if enforcement team visited which seems contradictory
insight: Customer appreciated advisor's politeness but was dissatisfied with payment plan options offered and contradictory information about prepaid meter eligibility.

author: Tracy
body: exceptional *****
insight: User is extremely satisfied with the app or service, though specific reasons for satisfaction are not detailed.

author: Scilla Shea
body: Im glad I changed but I was told I would get weekends half price and havent had anything in writing.
insight: Customer switched providers based on a verbal promise of weekend discounts but received no written confirmation of the offer.

author: Sandra Hawes
body: I am having trouble trying to ALTER to choose own 8 x 1hr slots for half price electricity now, submitting by the 25th of each month for the next month, and/or beyond.  I get error message constantly, so was unable to amend as wanted 🤔🥺🤷‍♀️
insight: User unable to modify electricity plan options due to persistent error messages in the app when attempting to select half-price electricity slots.

author: Mark Tritton
body: Easy & quick to use, saves having to phone readings through.
insight: User appreciates the app's convenience for submitting meter readings without needing to contact customer service.

author: CAROL BROWN
body: cant login not even with my finger print. Doesnt recognise my account number and email address even though I have just had a email from them to say my ballance is low. Chat is a waste of time as its a bot. Nothing but hassle. Reset my password and 4 links later its saying all links have been used already. WASTE OF TIME. Don't have a card to top up so dont know what to do tried ringing cant get threw.
insight: User is unable to access their account due to authentication failures and ineffective customer support options.

author: Irene Court
body: I contacted Scottish power regarding my direct debit. I have had nothing  but hassle from moving from one property to another. I’ve had numerous people calling me about 4 times a day regarding payment of final bill of old property. Someone was supposed to call me back yesterday but I’m still waiting. Not happy and I get people who can’t understand me on the phone as they are foreign and I can’t understand them so makes calls even more frustrating when speaking.
insight: Customer experienced repeated unwanted calls about final bill payment and poor communication due to language barriers with support staff.

author: Jill Gardiner
body: I'm reasonabley satisfied. Would be nice if the numbers are lower like before covid. And I had a smart meter reader I could see and understand.
insight: Customer is satisfied overall but wants lower bills and clearer smart meter data visualization in the app.

author: Brer Nigam
body: Does the work.. happy with it
insight: User is satisfied with the app's core functionality and performance.

author: Jayne Furey
body: its made my life so much easier to top up and keep track of what I'm using
insight: User appreciates the app's convenience for managing prepayment top-ups and monitoring energy consumption.

author: Nick Cook
body: Works fine...No issues.
insight: User is satisfied with the app's overall functionality and stability.

author: Nicholas Francis
body: Why doesn't the app give any Android Notification when any of the Power Saver Scheduled Periods or Live Events occur? It means I always miss them!
insight: User is missing Power Saver Scheduled Periods and Live Events because the app fails to send Android notifications for these important alerts.

author: Beverly Scarff
body: useless
insight: User has a fundamentally negative experience with the app but provides no specific details about what functionality or feature is causing their dissatisfaction.

author: Kimberliee Peters
body: it has all of a sudden stopped letting me log on to this app and I have no gas at all and have been cut off! I tried to call the company and the line cut me off saying can't help.
insight: Customer lost gas supply and cannot access the app to resolve the issue, with additional frustration from unhelpful customer service.

author: Anonymous
body:
She was full of information, but she spoke too quickly and I felt she was high pressure selling.  I have decided to make use of the 14day period in which you can back out.
I hope you will re-train your employee so that she speaks more slowly and doesn't make customers feel pressurised into buying.
I am still waiting for the confirmation, so that I can make use of the 14 day period in which we can back out.
insight: Customer felt pressured by aggressive sales tactics and is exercising their right to cancel within the 14-day cooling-off period.

author: ampelio
body: it was quick and easy to change the tariff to a new deal
insight: User found the tariff change process straightforward and efficient within the app.

author: Charlene
body: Had nothing but issues with Scottish power !
insight: Customer has experienced multiple unspecified problems with Scottish Power's service.

author: Denise Bannon
body: Unfortunately, the app is not working. I am unable to login, cannot check balance or credit account and I'm just being sent around in an eternal endless loop. Cache and settings all cleared. Latest app used but still cannot log in, as other users have also reported.
insight: User cannot access core app functions due to a login failure that persists despite troubleshooting attempts and affects multiple users.

author: Mr . Pearson
body: I've been using Scottish Power for 15+ years. I find them a good reliable supplier. The only issues I've had has been customer service contact. This doesn't go smoothly sometimes, better in recent years with things like webchat when you get passed the bot.
insight: Long-term customer appreciates reliability but notes that customer service contact methods have historically been inconsistent, though improving with digital options like webchat.

author: Michael H
body: Finally got an email to  get in touch
insight: User is satisfied after finally receiving a response from customer service via email.

author: Gianni S.
body: Good for me.
insight: User expresses general satisfaction with the app.

author: Carolina Vika
body: Customer service was really satisfied.
insight: User expresses satisfaction with the customer service experience provided by ScottishPower.

author: Malcolm Pratt
body: Advisor was more than willing to listen and help.
insight: User praised an advisor for their attentiveness and helpfulness in resolving their concern.

author: Anita Johnson
body: I been with you some years and you have almost got it right still room for improvement
insight: Long-term customer satisfied with service overall but identifies room for improvement in the app or service delivery.

author: Christopher Layton Blackham
body:
If i could opt for no 
star i would. I have never dealt with a more impersonal, inept company. I have had a complaint o/s fo 6 months and although SP owe me over £2,000 which they do not dispute they can’t get this paid to me!!!!
insight: Customer has an unresolved billing complaint lasting 6 months with an undisputed £2,000 owed but unpaid, citing poor complaint handling and impersonal service.

author: Jane
body: The man on phone was very helpful but he needs not to repeat him self more than 2 times  thanks for helping
insight: Customer appreciated helpful phone support but found the representative repetitive in their explanations.

author: Anonymous
body: Good so far . Everything’s works and customer service have been in contact to make sure all is ok .
insight: User is satisfied with app functionality and proactive customer service support.

author: JEAN RUSSELL
body: Pleasant assistant on thephone
insight: User had a positive experience with phone support staff professionalism and helpfulness.

author: MARIAN SAVVA - ANDREOU
body: Never sure if I’m on best rate. Hate having to do everything on line 😡
insight: User is uncertain about their energy tariff and frustrated by the lack of non-digital support options.

author: Mr Archibald
body: There used to be a function to take pictures of your meter reading, where has this gone? It was very handy
insight: User is dissatisfied that a previously useful meter reading photo feature has been removed from the app.

author: Mr John Marsh
body: Personelcontact exelent
insight: User had a positive experience with personal customer contact support despite minor spelling errors in their review.

author: William Hamer
body: Found the meter reading  box which sits in the house didn't work from the beginning, customer service where very unhelpful and couldn't explain a reason or give any advice on how to get a replacement other then that all works well
insight: Smart meter device failed from the start and customer service provided inadequate support in resolving the issue.

author: isabella elanko
body: Good still waiting fir the call to fix the problem
insight: User is satisfied with the app but frustrated by delayed response to their reported problem.

author: Mr James Hay-Barr
body: I am experiencing high night rate usage despite only using a fridge freezer and a TV. Is the heat exchanger causing this?
insight: Customer reports unusually high night rate charges and suspects a technical fault with their heat exchanger or meter readings.

author: Sharon Pinder
body: Never had a problem with Scottish power until now. They have not only taken 2 hours off the 50% weekend electricity but now you have to go into your account every month to tell them what hour you want on what days . I chose to keep mine next month at  weekends but  one week it starts at 12 and ends at the final week in the month at 3/4 who wants to do their washing that late in the day . Not only that who wants to keep logging in  and keep changing day and times ? I have better things to do like look for another utility company! Re reply Have no idea what you are talking about confused reply!
insight: Customer is frustrated with changes to their Economy 7 electricity plan requiring monthly manual adjustments to peak hours, which creates inconvenience and poor timing for household activities.

author: Sharon Y
body: Called up with a problem topping up my smart meter sorted it in 10 minutes thank you
insight: Customer praised quick and effective resolution of a smart meter top-up issue within 10 minutes.

author: Gillian Patricia Tayler
body: Getting someone to speak is a no no!
insight: User is frustrated with difficulty reaching customer support through the app.

author: Tom Telfer
body: Helpfull and frendly,easy to use
insight: User appreciates the app's user-friendly interface and helpful customer service experience.

author: Pammy Louise Smith
body: so i found a way to log back in from seeimg the reviews that people cant log back in u have to uninstall the app and re log back in and it worked fine for me
insight: User discovered a workaround for login issues by reinstalling the app, which resolved their authentication problem.

author: DAVID FRENCH
body:
Terrible company complaint from 7th march ESTIMATED BILL £580
STAY AWAY
insight: Customer is extremely dissatisfied with an unresolved complaint from March regarding an unexpectedly high estimated bill of £580.

author: Mr Salvati
body: Never have any issues with scottish power
insight: User expresses satisfaction with ScottishPower's overall service reliability and performance.

author: Mr Stephan Arts
body: Offering a full range of service. Gas, electricity and boiler service. Only slow when it comes down to smart meter for gas and not following up the offer for installing air pump
insight: Customer appreciates the broad service range but experienced delays with smart meter gas installation and inadequate follow-up on air pump installation offers.

author: Steven
body: All of a sudden I can't login to the app. I've tried to reset my passport, I keep getting a "sorry this link has already been used" My password is correct, I can log into the website with it, I just can't access my account on the app whatsoever. "Oops something went wrong". Please don't reply with generic "contact us" messages. If you check other reviews this is a common problem for multiple customers. I now have to call Scottish power to make payments to my smart meter.
insight: User is locked out of the app due to a broken password reset function and recurring authentication errors, forcing them to use alternative channels for account management.

author: Mrs Hetherington
body: I have been trying to get a refund on my account as I’m over £800 in credit at it’s being nothing but hassle. At the end of the day it’s MY money and not there’s. Now they are saying I have to wait another 22days ( why) I already read my meters and got my bill and there saying there is an error so it will take 22 days to fix yeah right, if I owed you money it would be a different scenario
insight: Customer is frustrated with a prolonged 22-day delay to process a refund of £800 credit and perceives unfair treatment compared to how the company would handle a debt owed to them.

author: Ms Nicola Brown
body: Nothing was resolved after 4 weeks and three phone calls I still have no idea if my electricity smart meter is working. I have had to do everything manually on line including asking for a refund of over £300 as my account was so in credit. Customer service representatives communications were poor, with both attitude and language barriers , they had no idea of previous conversations making it very frustrating.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged unresolved issues with smart meter functionality and poor customer service across multiple contacts, compounded by account credit management problems.

author: Jan
body: My electricity smart meter wasn’t compatible with my gas smart meter. It took the  4 months approximately to replace my electricity meter and I’m know delighted with the outcome. They do email you every week to keep you informed which is good but one of them tried to tell me it was working and I just couldn’t see it!!!!
insight: Customer experienced a 4-month delay replacing an incompatible electricity smart meter and was given misleading information by staff about its functionality.

author: Sarah Mcknight
body: I can not log so there for can not able to see the money I have on my meter or top up it a joke I contact the ombudsman and I'm not wasting my time trying to sort out why nobody can asses the app or logs them out
insight: User is unable to log into the app to view prepay balance and top up, has escalated to ombudsman due to lack of support resolution.

author: Manuela D
body: It worked OK in till now, I can't get in the App and I need to top up, my credit is running out, I clean catch, I rest the app, nothing, "ups something went wrong" this is the message.
insight: User is unable to access the app to top up their prepayment credit due to login and crash errors.

author: Nicola Dyson
body: Scottish Power closed my account 2 months ago without instruction or reason.  I am still battling to get my account opened again, after hours of telephone calls. Further, they have registered my account to some else and sent the bill to my address in the name of another person.  A new account has now been opened for the gas but, despite sending evidence that I am still in the property, I do not have an account for my electricity and nor am I able to pay it.  I am very disappinted after 15 years of really good service.
insight: Customer's account was unexpectedly closed and subsequently misallocated to another person, resulting in inability to pay bills and requiring extensive contact attempts to resolve after 15 years of loyalty.

author: elisabeth sheehan
body:
When I click on ‘confirmation of your new energy tariff’ in my In Box the envelope icon circle goes round for a few seconds and then goes back to the envelope and I can’t access my new energy tariff. I tried to phone but your lines were busy. I would be really grateful if you could send me a paper confirmation by letter. I am 82 and would really appreciate it. Account 16040211190
Thank you
insight: User cannot access their new energy tariff confirmation in the app due to a technical issue, and unable to reach customer support by phone, requesting paper documentation instead.

author: Linda Walker
body: can't log in at all with biometrics or manually. It's a disgrace. how am I supposed to top up?
insight: User is unable to access their account through either biometric or manual login, preventing them from topping up their energy credit.

author: MNB
body: On pay as you go tariff sent a bill to pay. How it possible on pay as you go. I already paid all my energy in advance. Rip off people. Avoid. Scam
insight: Customer on pay-as-you-go tariff received an unexpected bill despite having prepaid for energy, perceiving it as a scam or billing error.

author: Tam Meechan
body: the best 👌
insight: User expresses high satisfaction with the app or service overall.

author: Billy Hall
body:
Always pushing the smart meter 
Lots folk have horror things to say
I for one won't.
Have it
insight: Customer is refusing to accept a smart meter installation despite acknowledging others have had negative experiences.

author: Tamas Gungl
body: It doesn't work, I haven't been able to pay online for days.
insight: User is unable to complete online payments due to a multi-day app functionality failure.

author: Jayz Clembo
body: pure pish can't login keeps saying details wrong when there not a really need to top up on app same problem different day 😕
insight: User is unable to log in due to persistent authentication errors and finds the top-up feature problematic.

author: kerry stark
body: absolutely awful app, always issues
insight: User experiences frequent technical issues with the app that severely impact their experience.

author: Pabs
body: Got told to expect a cheque on the 18th or the 19th of this month in regards to dishonest theft and misuse of my details, have email correspondence confirming this, now this scum company has jus emailed, said they've credited my Scottish power account with 40 pounds! Brilliant! Except I'm not a customer of yours and your agent stole my details and signed me up to a contract despite me explicitly saying I didn't want that, so, this clown show of a company have emailed me ,told me to expect a cheque on the 18th or 19th of march, then told me they've credited my Scottish power account with 40 pounds, then in the same email,said they can't process it further,as I don't have a Scottish power account..... Make it make sense.... I don't have a Scottish power account,BECAUSE I HAD TO CANCEL THE CONTRACT YOUR LYING AGENT STOLE MY DETAILS AND SIGNED ME UP TO AGAINST MY EXPLICIT WISHES NOT TO.hey,Scottish power, still got the picture of your dishonest agent who stole my details, would you like me to email the pic again to you?
insight: Customer claims an agent fraudulently created an account using stolen personal details and the company's compensation process is inconsistent and illogical.

author: Theresa Kinsella
body: ive been trying all day to top up my smart metre, I can't login to my account my email address is correct and my password so how am I supposed to put electric on I've no other way of topping up. tried ringing and thats a waste of time too.
insight: Customer unable to top up their smart meter due to login failure and unresponsive customer support, leaving them without an alternative solution.

author: PAUL SPENCER
body: You didn't give me my £100 loyalty for staying with you
insight: Customer did not receive an expected £100 loyalty reward despite remaining with the company.

author: Kay Merry
body: app isn't working, reset won't work. can't see my credit balance
insight: User cannot access their credit balance due to app malfunction and failed reset attempts.

author: stuhar65
body: After the last update I cannot login app is a disgrace and looking through the reviews it's been doing this for years with no resolution I advise everyone to use a different supplier
insight: Critical login functionality has been broken since a recent update and remains unresolved despite years of complaints, prompting the user to recommend switching suppliers.

author: Alan
body: Bought a wall box and had problems for 2 years,had to use as a dumb charger as had connectivity issues.3 to 4 days response from wall box and generic responces.was second charger as first one did not work at all (red flag?)tried to get my 1 k back from Scottish power who sold me this junk.got delays delays then getting hanged up on - seriously !!!!! .so ended up buying a hyper volt -Great charger works as advertised.now I have disconnected the rubbish wall box they are using this as an excuse not to refund me after selling me a charger not  fit for purpose.seriously bad company Scottish power and the Spanish wall box .you have been warned .do not buy .
insight: Customer experienced persistent connectivity issues with a purchased wall box charger and faced unresponsive customer service and refusal to refund a product deemed unfit for purpose.

author: Bola Imran
body: headache app
insight: User finds the app frustrating and difficult to use overall.

author: diane harrison
body: what a experience with the customer service department unprofessional still not resolved my problem never phone you back i have phoned over 50 times the phone gets put down someone answered and was speaking a language i didn’t understand and laughing still no further on after six weeks it’s so unprofessional from scottish power and you can’t get help it’s a call centre sent emails gone onto social media aswell i won’t hold me breath for someone to get back me
insight: Customer experienced severe frustration with unresponsive customer service across multiple channels, including language barriers and lack of follow-up over a six-week period.

author: Gemma Agar
body: App has completely broken since the last update. Can no longer log in as I just get stuck in a loop of verification emails and error messages. Very frustrating as I need to top up urgently
insight: User is unable to log in due to a verification email loop and error messages following a recent app update, preventing them from topping up their account urgently.

author: Amanda Everson
body: Very expensive and sarcastic staff who don't help people who are struggling
insight: Customer is frustrated with high costs and perceives staff as unhelpful and dismissive towards those experiencing financial hardship.

author: Jonathan Fryer
body: Multiple conversation since January for fitting of a gas smart meter nothing done about it no explanation left without a gas top up card as supposed to be moving onto direct debit 5 hour chat than 2 hour chat just to get an engineer to top up Y gas that was off for 4 days.  Vulnerable disabled customer.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged delays in smart meter installation, poor communication, service failures affecting vulnerable disabled person, and inadequate support through multiple lengthy chats.

author: Andrew  George
body: The call handler who handled my call was helpful and efficient and cancelled our application to swich. However, the salesperson who approched me at ASDA Tonypandy, South Wales blatantly lied to me and gave incorrect information to gain the sale. He promised Scottish power would be cheaper than my British Gas supply. It wasn't.  He promised me that Scottish power would pay my exit fees and this was also untrue. Scottish power told me  they would investigate. I will not consider using this company in the future.
insight: Customer was misled by an in-store salesperson about pricing and exit fee coverage, resulting in financial loss and loss of trust in the company.

author: skip
body: Scottish Power website is completely broken. Reset password links not working, view meter readings not working, view final bill not working. I have wasted 3 days and still ongoing to sort things with them, things that you would normally do on the website for a few minutes (if the company has a working website of course). Is anything on this website working?
insight: Multiple critical website features are non-functional, preventing the user from completing essential self-service tasks over several days.

author: Tamryn Steinschauer
body: First year I have signed up and found myself in a lot of debt as the monthly cost was not calculated correctly by them. However when you call customer services they fantastic to help and they provide alternative way to assist you in paying it back
insight: User experienced billing calculation errors leading to debt, but praised customer service for providing helpful resolution and payment options.

author: Lynne
body: Scottish power made a mess of my bills, I paid an outstanding balance before moving and SP kept chasing me for payment. Lost count of calls I've made. Absolute shambles. Terrible customer service, I would prefer to give zero stars which would be more representative of my experience
insight: Customer experienced billing errors after account closure and received inadequate support despite multiple contact attempts.

author: mies j k
body: App easy to use x
insight: User appreciates the intuitive design and ease of use of the ScottishPower app.

author: Alexa Wren
body: After being swayed by a sales agent, my wife and I switched to Scottish Power. The sales agent looked at our current usage and promised we could pay less with Scottish Power. We switched on 3rd December and received our first bill on the 14th. We were shocked to see that they were charging over £200 for 10 days worth of energy. We made an enquiry and got nothing back until next months bill where they were trying to tell us we’d used over 2000kWh for a month, that’s more than we used in the previous year altogether! We complained and asked this to be looked into, only to be met with false promise of call backs, misdirection of what the problem was, and what felt like patronising blame on the last phone call. They are insisting that we’re now £2k in debt in the space of 3 months and no one seems to be willing to even investigate the possibility that their calculations are incorrect! It’s a 2 person household where we both work in an office 5 days a week, it’s impossible to have used that much energy! I wish we’d never switched and will be switching back to our old provider as soon as our complaint is closed. We’ve already gone to the ombudsman, hopefully they can help!
insight: Customer disputes unusually high energy charges and claims of excessive usage, but receives inadequate investigation and support from Scottish Power, leading to escalation to the ombudsman.

author: Jacqueline Dixon
body: I feel they did not listen when I first made the complaint. I also think thier sales team need to be retrained to avoid misleading customer's instead of worrying more about there sales results.
insight: Customer feels unheard during complaint resolution and believes sales team uses misleading practices rather than prioritizing customer interests.

author: Mr Thompson
body: Put the payment up many times now in debt don’t use them
insight: Customer is in debt due to repeated payment increases and explicitly advises against using the company.

author: Disgruntled customer
body: Awful, denied responsibility, couldn’t understand half of what was said to me due to accent. And am still being charged for has going to the wrong meter….. useless I highly recommend you look elsewhere for your energy
insight: Customer experienced poor billing accuracy and ineffective customer service communication, leading to dissatisfaction with the provider.

author: Ms Cindy Yandell
body: This is based on my overall experience of scottish power. They have the odd people who are extremely helpful and understanding but most should never be in customer service. I have been unfortunate to speak to some very unhelpful and nasty (the only way to describe them) people who have done nothing to help. One even called me a liar without actually saying the words. Disgraceful. When problems are sorted, they have in my experience gone back on it causing more anxiety and upset, even threatening me at times. I would never recommend scottish power to anyone after what I have been through with them
insight: Customer experienced consistently poor and unprofessional service from staff, with unresolved issues being reversed and causing additional distress.

author: J Smith
body: The adviser promised to ring ms back early this morning but up to know not heard from her m lm. I am venerable and discontinued help.
insight: Vulnerable customer left waiting for promised callback from adviser, feeling abandoned and unsupported.

author: Sue
body:
I would not touch Scottish Power - the customer service is totally not to any standard that is acceptable.  The most unhelpful company I have ever dealt with. Give you different information each time you call - very rude - do not listen to you -  cannot give you a straight or accurate answer - talk over you. Must have spent 10 hrs on various calls - now been on hold - this call is 45 mins - all I want to do is get away from this company!

Just transferred over to Octopus - would give them 5 stars what a difference. Well done Octopus you have shown how to look after a customer which Scottish Power need to heed.
insight: Customer experienced consistently poor customer service with long hold times, inconsistent information, and rude staff, leading them to switch to a competitor.

author: C Barton
body: Very frustrating when call handler dies not listen properly and pass on specific and relevant information. This has cost me a 3 month delay in getting the issue fixed.
insight: Poor call handler communication and failure to relay information accurately resulted in a three-month delay in issue resolution.

author: customer
body: I been with Scotish power boiler ensurance, pls save your sanity and do not take a plan with them. A few times i needed their help and they have not helped us, when ypu talk to their staff they ask you a few times the same question, they put you trough random departments, never a straight answer, not worth getting a ensurance with them.
insight: Customer experienced poor service with boiler insurance, including repeated questions, department transfers, and lack of helpful support.

author: Owleyes
body: I have been a Scottish Power customer for many years. Although I feel reasonably happy I do find their customer service to be somewhat lacking. Having to talk to an automated Bot online to resollve a problem, instead of a real person, is never ideal. On the plus side their App and Web sites are perfect for me to manage my account. Sending meter readings in is easy and saves me from the hassle, disruption and possible problems of having a smart meter fitted.

author: Terry Crawford
body:
I have had the displeasure of being a customer of Scottish Power since 14 January, and can honestly say I have seen some of the worst administrational processes, harassment, and mis-management I have experienced as a customer. 
As the face of Scottish Power, the call centre really do not represent this company at all well (but read on). I have had 6 calls with the customer service advisors since 27 February to dispute an 'estimated' bill (for an unoccupied property. The bill is 2-times greater than my own home bill with a family of 4).  
I have been effectively misinformed twice about how the bill has been derived, and that my request to be escalated to the complaints department (on 6 March) has been actioned.
During my lengthy call on 6 March with the billing department, the operator was unable to explain how the bill had been calculated.  Additionally, I was initially told that the final meter reading had been physically provided by the tenant to then be told it had been automatically generated (read on). A hardline approach was adopted to try and get me to pay, which I resisted, and I was informed that, despite my complaint, I would continue to receive calls about the bill, and to ignore this contact.  It was stated, it would take 7-10 working days to hear from the compliant's team.
Since 6 March, I have received 31 calls and 11 text messages from Scottish Power.  Following their advice, I ignored most calls and messages, but when I did pick up (mainly out of frustration), without checking my account, the advisor went straight into their scripts requesting me to pay the bill.  Hugely intrusive and annoying.
The icing on the cake, was receiving an email last night threatening me with bailiff action.
Whilst typing this review, I have been on the phone to Scottish Power for 85 minutes (on hold for 40 minutes) in an effort to resolve the matter. I have been informed that my complaint that was lodged on 6 March actually hasn't been lodged, so I need to wait a further 7-10-working days to to hear back.  To stop and calls, texts, and threat of legal action, I have requested that my billing process is put of hold until I hear from the complaints team. I was initially informed that they do not have the ability to do this. Ridiculous right? You dispute an estimated bill, which is grossly incorrect, but you are still expected to pay it in the interim whilst the complaints team contact you. Bullyboy tactics in my opinion.
Having spoken to the Supervisor, Naveed, who has agreed to pause the billing process until my complaint is resolved, and cease all contact from the billing team, he was excellent, and his colleague Inga was lovely.  I wish I had spoken with both these individuals at the start of the process. During this call, it was confirmed that the final meter reading was physically provided by the tenant, contrary to one conversation on 6 March. I have been informed that my complaint has now been lodged and I will hear from them in 7-10 days (give or take).
I look forward to hearing from the complaints department in the hope that this horrendous situation can be resolved. 
For directors reading this review, please listen to the call recordings and you will understand my angst.  Happy to discuss your processes and customer service, which are hugely inefficient.
insight: Customer experienced severe billing disputes, poor call centre service, and aggressive collection tactics, only resolved through escalation to a supervisor.

Them got a long phone call where nothing was resolved at all. Now have been moved onto official complaint as operator refused too check account info.more nonsense

author: Transform Technology LImited
body:
Communicating with Scottish power is HOPELESS! 
Their overseas? 'Help centre' is USELESS!

I tried for months to get any sense out of Scottish Power. 

In the end, I asked our local MP to intervene -- which he did and we finally gor a human to talk to!

The Problem: Our SMART meter didn't work. 

I raised a formal complaint. 

After weeks of waiting and missed appointments, they finally got a chap to come and add the 'Telemetry' unit to the so-called SMART meter -- on the second attempt as the first time, he didn't have the authority to add the unit, despite having them in his van! -- so had to go away and we had to wait another week for him to return to add the 4G telemetry device. 

After a further 2 weeks we all concluded it still didn't work!

Another Technician came and replaced everything -- SMART meter and Telemetry unit. This time it worked.

Lead time was around 2 months to get to this point.

We have a Heat Pump and wanted a reduced cost Heat pump tariff -- only to be told this week (24th March, now the SMART meter is working) that all heat pump tariffs have been withdrawn!
So the only option was to 'fix for 12 months.'
 
Goodness knows if and when they will introduce another Heat Pump Tariff.

The one glimmer of hope with Scottish Power is that once I raised a Formal Complaint and got a named complaint handler, communications improved and the problem of the SMART meter was resolved -- eventually!

Without the Customer going out of thier way to achive communication, Scottish Power's Customer Service is as close to 'Useless' as it is possible to get!

No one takes any Resposnsibility to solve the Customer's problem! You get passed from one unknowledgable person to the next -- until you put the phone down in frustration!

They don't have enough English speaking staff with sufficient understanding to be able to deal with a problem!

The on-line chat it worthless.

In truth, they don't appear to have enough staff and their 'on the road' contractor initally gave us a lead time of 6 months to change the SMART meter!
In reality the timescale was 2 months -- but only after I kept pushing them for a sensible date!

Until you raise a Formal Complaint, Scottish Power believe the problem is YOU!

We should not have to put up with such appalling service.
insight: Customer experienced a 2-month resolution process for a faulty smart meter installation compounded by poor communication, unhelpful overseas support staff, and lack of accountability across the organisation.

author: Kerry
body:
Title: Business account not set up – left with charges and no control

We are a business operating from 32 The Shambles, Worcester, and our experience with ScottishPower has been extremely poor.

From the start, we notified them that we had taken over the premises, provided full company details and meter readings, and requested an account to be set up correctly in our company name.

Despite this, ScottishPower failed to set up the account properly and refused to accept payments. This left us in a position where we were actively using electricity but had no account, no agreed tariff, and no way to manage or reduce any charges.

Over a period of approximately three months, charges were allowed to build up while we had no control over the situation. We were also unable to switch supplier during this time.

To make matters worse, debt collection correspondence has been sent relating to a previous occupier of the premises, which we have no connection to. We have had to formally dispute this.

We consider this completely unfair. If the account had been set up correctly from day one, we would have been able to manage the account, make payments, and choose an appropriate supplier.

This issue has now been formally raised as a complaint and escalated further.

Based on our experience, the handling of business accounts, particularly change of occupancy, needs significant improvement. Customers should not be left without control of their account while charges continue to accumulate.
insight: ScottishPower failed to properly set up a business account during change of occupancy, leaving the customer without control while charges accumulated and unable to switch suppliers.

body: Scottish Power is like all the big power companies greedy and full of red tape. They provide electricity which I cannot fault. I have a steady supply. Occasionally there are storms causing power outage but they seem, for me anyway, to be repaired quickly. I did recently change tariff and it was very complicated and confusing. Also I got a smart meter installated last year and that caused the tariff change to be more complex than was needed as well. I wouldn't say they were a terrible company.  But confusing tariffs and lack of communication between departments let them down. I stuck with them ,because the tariff ,when down to it, wasn't any more expensive than any other company. I wouldn't put anyone off using them but..... dont expect 1st class customer service.
insight: Core service delivery is reliable but confusing tariffs, poor interdepartmental communication, and smart meter installation complexity undermine the customer experience.

author: Andree Gaskill
body:
I have scored 3 for my overall experience. However on my last call to the Company the Advisor Layla actually knew what she was doing and treated my call with empathy and professionalism.  It had taken many calls previously before my queries were sorted, 5 in total.

Think the company need to re-visit their bereavement policy and maybe amend it so that the bereaved families are not bombarded with letters and threats of taking to matter further especially if they are aware the estate is being dealt with by a Solicitors. in this regard they just do not seem to care or show compassion. The telephone operators seem to follow a script.
insight: Customer experienced poor service across multiple calls and criticizes insensitive bereavement policy, though praised one advisor named Layla for professionalism.

author: Lee
body: Not helpful im struggling with bills im suffering really bad with my mental health my mum just past away im waiting on my full benefits yo come in I get 340 s month that's for shopping and bills its impossible iv asked for help as I have solar panels
insight: Customer in financial hardship following bereavement is struggling with unaffordable bills despite having solar panels and requesting assistance.

author: Heather Prouse
body: brilliant

author: Martin DDZ

author: Paula Moore
body: You tell me prices are going down,but my direct debit as gone up.

author: HARRIET PARROTT
body:
Absolutely awful company full of liars that will say anything just to get you to agree to set up a direct debit. I have a debt outstanding with them through no fault of my own (the meter that was installed before I moved in was t registered correctly and wasn't giving readings) I agreed to pay the debt at 48.85 a month and my monthly usage at 77.63 (this is much more than I actually use) total of £126.48 . I've just looked at my onl8ne account and they're set to debit £193.52
I've continually messaged the agent "Gary"who has now been ignoring my messages for the past 2 weeks.
I have the amounts i agreed to pay in writing and I will not be paying a penny more. I have cancelled the direct debits and I will be making manual payments. If you don't like it then sort out the flipping direct debit so it's correct. This issue has been going on for 2 years. I have fibromyalgia and am currently extremely ill because of this issue dragging on and the constant worry! You will get fake understanding and sympathy up until you give you bank details then they'll mug you off as soon as ignore you
insight: Customer alleges ScottishPower set up incorrect direct debit amounts despite written agreement, and customer service agent has been unresponsive for two weeks.

author: William Stratton

Sent pictures and messages to the company who have zero interest in sorting the problem. Terrible service and operator avoid at all cost.
insight: App fails to process top-ups correctly and transfer credit to smart meter since November update, with company unresponsive to support requests.

insight: Unauthorized direct debit was taken from non-customer's savings account and three months of unresolved complaints with repetitive questioning have led to ombudsman escalation.

body: Hi to be honest I’m 73 and I’m not good with technical things so these app I find a challenge for me! Just recently I need to find information about my usage! It took 48hrs for a reply! It same endless headache 🤕

author: Catherine

Eventually I actually got a replacement meter and it’s worked really well no more power cuts shower the IHD didn’t pair with the meter and when I complained I was told they were out of stock. Thus was months ago - still no IHD.
insight: Customer experienced a 2-year delay in resolving smart meter issues and power cuts, and remains without a replacement IHD despite months of waiting.

author: Gill
body: Second time writing about Scottish power regarding warm energy grants ,my son was on key meter but decided for disability sake to have a smart meter fitted. Had trouble getting it to top up so had to do it on the phone manually. Not good when your Son is non verbal Autistic, as we where on the phone his discount letter came , she asked me for the bar code and said it will be added within a couple of days it's now 3 weeks down the line . Just rung them up they said I could have taken the letter and topped up with the car code and added it to the meter that way . But we got cut of and had to ring back again , been told completely different yet again now he has to wait till the end of the mth now . Absolute joke being told lots of disinformation drives me to the point of being ill , sorting someone bills out that can't do it for themselves is made so difficult.
insight: Customer received inconsistent information and poor support when attempting to apply a warm energy grant discount to their non-verbal autistic son's smart meter account, resulting in unresolved billing issues after three weeks.

This still isn't fixed 3 weeks now?

body:
Absolute shambles. Spent ages online signing up for the EV charger rate. Completed every detail then... Nothing. 
Poor guy on the helpline, really did his best, he could see I'd completed the application but all he could do with their lousy system was delete it and told me to try again in "5-7 days."

What a mess!

author: Malcolm Newton
body: Still waiting for a connection for the smart meter to work. You say you are receiving readings, then why do you keep asking me to submit readings
insight: Customer is frustrated by inconsistent smart meter data handling, with the app claiming to receive readings while still requesting manual submissions.

author: Douglas Cobb
body:
I switched to ScottishPower from EDF not long ago, and it has been the biggest mistake of my life. Since the switch, I have been harassed relentlessly—text messages, emails, phone calls—nonstop. If it’s not a text, it’s a call; if it’s not a call, it’s a letter. I have never encountered such aggressive payment chasing in my life. Constant calls, even from call centers abroad, all demanding payment for a trivial amount—around £100 for gas and electricity. I have mental health issues, and this constant bombardment has been absolutely unbearable.

In a bid to end this harassment, I spent over an hour on the phone to switch to prepayment—just to settle the small balance. But after all that effort, they admitted they couldn’t switch my gas supply, because their smart meter doesn’t detect it. Now, I’m left with a messed-up system—50% prepay, 50% direct debit—complete chaos. I asked for a technician to check the meter, but was told there are no appointments. They don’t care. They are in breach of regulations and they know it. This company has been an absolute nightmare. Do not—under any circumstances—switch to ScottishPower. You will regret it.
insight: Customer experienced aggressive payment collection tactics and failed smart meter installation resulting in a dysfunctional hybrid payment system with unresponsive support.

author: John Dunn
body: email replies seem automated, and call centre people can be difficult to understand. Pulled fixed term deals from the website without explanation (e.g. Heat Pump Saver Tariff) for which the reason was later given as the current middle east crisis.
insight: Poor communication quality with automated email responses, hard-to-understand call centre staff, and unexplained removal of fixed-term tariff options without adequate reasoning.

The app no longer looks the same as it used to and doesn’t tell me the information I found useful.
insight: User experienced difficulties accessing their account due to an incorrect email address on file, compounded by poor customer service responsiveness and dissatisfaction with recent app interface changes.

Terrible company

I used to beabke to look at my account and see payments made and charges for gas and electricity on the same statement. I can no longer do this .  C

author: hywel williams
body: I have been with SP for over 6 years and they have been OK. The people in customer services are always good when you get through. What made me switch was the fact that they kept hassling me to get a smart meter. I was happy to have one, but like 1000s of others in the  UK, the phone signal near the meter is too weak, so their engineer said it was not possible. But that did not stop the constant calls - I suspect it was from a linked company that was paid on achieving a switch target. Never mind how many times I told them that their engineer said it was not possible, the calls kept coming - just lazy automated lack of corporate memory. So 10/10 for the really nice customer services managers, 2/10 for the hidden managers who made a decision to allow others to badger customers for something that was not possible
insight: Customer switched providers due to persistent unwanted smart meter installation calls despite an engineer confirming it was technically impossible, despite positive experiences with direct customer service staff.

Every single consumer in the UK Do Not become a customer of Scottish Power.
They're incompetent, they manufacture fraudulent gas & electricity bills and then sell the fraudulent debt to a company called " Jefferson's".

author: Pauline Smith
body:
Totally incompetent complaints handler. Been having issues with our electricity bill since we got an EV charger  installed via Scottish Power on 8th Jan. Our complaint was closed without a resolution. Still waiting on this useless company to deal with our complaint, 10 weeks of phone calls to no avail. Numerous phone calls made and time waisted on this issue. 


Edit * Here is the update you promised. Saying nothing that hasn't been said before. Note that our complaint has now been reopened after your agent closed it without a resolution. 

Dear Mrs Smith,

Complaint number: 

I hope this email finds you well. Please be advised that I am still actively addressing your complaint. The matter has been escalated to the relevant department, and I am currently awaiting their response. I sincerely appreciate your patience and cooperation during this process. Rest assured, I will update you as soon as I receive any information regarding your concerns. Should you have any further questions in the meantime, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Please reply to this email using the same subject line. This will ensure your response is sent directly to me, so I can resolve your complaint as quickly as possible.

Thanks in advance for your help.

Kind regards
KHALIPHA
ScottishPower Customer Care
insight: Customer frustrated with prolonged complaint handling over EV charger billing issues, with their complaint initially closed without resolution despite 10 weeks of contact attempts.

author: Clive Pritchard
body: As an 84 year old with limited IT skills, I found the experience frustrating and annoying. You seem incapable of giving an answer to anything on your webchat, with all other avenues cluttered with stuff I hadn't asked about. To me it was a totally user unfriendly, other than the lady I finally spoke with. She was both charming and informative.
insight: Digital channels are confusing and unhelpful for older, less tech-savvy users, though direct phone support staff provide excellent service.

author: H.B.M MONEY
body:
I would give zero stars if I could. I moved into a newly converted studio flat on December 23rd, 2025, and my experience with Scottish Power has been nothing short of a documented nightmare.
 
When I called to register my account and provide my opening meter readings, I was horrified to find that they already had my personal details—but with a completely incorrect date of birth. Worse still, they had me registered as the responsible tenant for ALL 11 studio flats in the entire building!
 
They are trying to charge me for a massive bill dating back to August 2025. During that time, I wasn't even living in North London; I was living in Southwark. Despite me providing clear evidence, including my signed tenancy agreement proving my move-in date was 23rd December 2025, they have been utterly unhelpful.
 
Most concerning is that they refuse to explain how they obtained my personal information, or why they assigned an entire commercial-to-residential conversion to one single person. Their records are a mess, their data handling is suspicious, and their customer service is non-existent.
 
It’s no wonder this company has such a toxic reputation across social media. I am not the only resident in this building disgusted by their incompetence. I am switching suppliers immediately.

 Do yourself a favor and stay far away from Scottish Power. They are a disgrace.
insight: Customer was incorrectly registered for multiple properties with wrong personal details and charged for periods before moving in, with inadequate resolution and data handling concerns.

author: Simon G
body: Was forced to use the complaints procedure as I could not get a straight answer to a simple question from the fit team. Whoever handled complaint did eventually  get the answer...but i fear even he struggled. Even now I am asking  questio ns of them because nothing was handled properly  in November  2025.  The fit enrolement process is an absolute nightmare to navigate. Even the people who answer the phone don't understand what they are talking about. I was warned by one of your staff that is a nightmare and he wasn't wrong.
insight: Customer experienced prolonged difficulties navigating the FIT enrollment process and obtaining answers from support staff, requiring formal complaints escalation.

author: PAUL WARD
body:
I have been in talks with Scottish Power over the past 6 weeks regarding my electricity account.
Suddenly #, after a regular amount over the past 6 years, my bill suddenly showed I owed £4111.00.
My heart sank. How did this happen?
after weeks of waiting and phone calls, they couldn't explain why this occurred, but instead, told me the bill was correct.
I looked into this myself and straight away, I saw the error.
They did an estimated reading. This was 65kw, that's it, my actual read submitted was roughly 99886kw. So, as you see, a huge error and that was the huge invoice. It was like going back to a new installation, but saying I owed the total amount from 65kw to the reading it is today. I have been with them for years. Terrible customer service, they are not trained and have no way of looking outside the box and investingating. Nobody has taken charge to see this through.


For the people who look into this account and can not see this error straight away, you should be sacked.
The account has been analysed, and still nobody saw this error at first glance.
The stress and worry has been unbearable.
insight: Customer was overcharged £4111 due to an estimated meter reading error that Scottish Power failed to identify or investigate properly despite weeks of contact.

UPDATE:

insight: Customer has been unable to complete account setup for 4 months despite multiple interactions with support staff, and is now prevented from switching providers due to an unresolved issue on a non-functional account.

I've unistalled/reinstalled the app numerous times,re-registered each time,cleared cache daily and restarted my phone after every action, it's sorts the biometrics issue for a few logins then goes back to the original issue of having to login manually.
insight: User is unable to use biometric login functionality despite multiple troubleshooting attempts and repeated contact with customer service.

author: Albert Imafiabor
body: very good 👍
insight: User expresses general satisfaction with the app or service.

author: Dave Perkins
body: I do not like talking to a machine. I find it difficult to use computer and phones, I would rather like to talk to a person. I am to old now for this modern world.
insight: User prefers human interaction over automated systems and finds digital interfaces difficult to navigate.

author: Emma  Law
body: The new monthly payment this year has raised 1/3 without an explanation.
insight: Customer's monthly payment increased by one-third without clear communication or explanation from the utility.

author: Sue Wall
body: I no longer have access to Settings on my SP app, it's just disappeared. I have therefore lost the ability to use fingerprint recognition, but can still log on with email address, password, and using the code sent to my email address. As far as I know, I've tried everything, reinstalling app etc. but no luck. The most frustrating thing is I can only occasionally speak to an agent, and the auto robot is absolutely useless.
insight: User lost access to app Settings and biometric login due to a technical issue, compounded by difficulty reaching helpful customer support.

author: Valerie Wells
body: I am happy with existing plan. .sorry in Hosp at the moment.

author: Christopher Twilley
body: Absolute bollocks. Cannot open the account on the app.
insight: User is unable to open their account on the mobile app, preventing access to their account information.

author: Mahrukh  Kamran

author: Phil Lynch

author: RAFIEL NORRIS
body: Excellent
insight: User expresses strong satisfaction with the app or service without specifying particular features.

author: Customer Maria-Chiara
body:
The phone operatives are always friendly and knowledgeable. Whilst they are on the phone to you they go to a great deal of trouble to sort issues out however, in my experience they never fulfill promises to phone back, including when they claim to have made a note in their diary to do so.
In my opinion Scottish Power are incompetent - it took them weeks to  connect my smart meter. After their assurances that I would no longer need to submit meter readings they messaged me to ask me tl do so. Email links they send me never work. I am also very aggrieved that they sent me a  cheque refund for an overpayment they took from me when their operator had given me a choice and I had requested the money be paid back into the account they took it from.
insight: While phone staff are friendly and helpful during calls, ScottishPower fails to follow through on promises, delivers poor digital experiences, and handles refunds incorrectly.

author: Joyce Pyper
body: great app once you know how's to work it.
insight: User finds the app functional and satisfactory once familiar with its interface, though acknowledges a steep learning curve.

author: Andy
body: Website remains flaky, eg tells me I am overdue a meter reading when it has in fact been submitted the previous week. Some analysis tools don't work at times
insight: Website has reliability issues with inaccurate meter reading status displays and intermittent analysis tool functionality.

author: RS S
body: The last update made the app refuce to stay logged in. It keeps trying to make me log into the browser. The only thing I can do is uninstall and reinstall. This allows me to login to the app again. It only lasts about 10m before logging me out and restarting the process. Please fix this.
insight: A recent app update has broken session persistence, forcing users to repeatedly log in every 10 minutes despite reinstalling the app.

author: tktktkmaxxxxxx
body: Why do the developers continually mess with things. I am now unable to submit meter readings via the app. Very poor and so annoying
insight: User is unable to submit meter readings through the app following recent updates, causing frustration with the app's functionality.

author: KOSHY JACOB
body: I tried to pay the balance online, for which I needed a password to log in. I tried to reset the password and waited for your -mail to do it but did not receive it. I tried two more times. No e-mail to reset the password. This is not the first time. I had this experience several times. When I receive  your questionnaire some times I had to log into my account. There again the question of password comes up.  The bad experience goes on!
insight: User is unable to reset their password as password reset emails are not being delivered, preventing them from accessing their account to pay bills.

author: karen jones
body: unable to use the power save feature, keeps saying to update the app, but even when I have, it keeps saying to update.
insight: User cannot access the power save feature due to a persistent app update bug that remains unresolved even after updating.

author: Mary Davidson
body: I phoned to enquire why I was required to provide a meter reading so shortly after my meter had been installed. I was satisfied with the explanation the advisor provided. I am still quite dissatisfied with my service
insight: Customer service adequately explained a meter reading requirement but overall service satisfaction remains poor.

author: Ronald Edwards
body: I have been trying to get into my app but every time I get a security code it is always invalid. Have received about 20 codes sent to me via email but all have been invalid.As a customer for sixty years it is not good enough. Please sort it out. I only use a 4 digit pin to access BT app. So why make it so complicated.
insight: User unable to access app due to repeated invalid security codes despite multiple attempts, frustrated by overly complex authentication process.

author: Terry Bowkett
body: round and round in circles.
insight: User expresses frustration with circular or repetitive processes, suggesting poor app navigation or unresolved issues.

author: Richard Veal
body: after update, keeps telling me a new version is available so update now.
insight: User experiences a frustrating update loop where the app repeatedly prompts for a new version after updating, preventing normal use.

author: Waltlev
body: Find the website annoying never accept the password, have to change it every time I log in .
insight: User experiences repeated authentication failures requiring password changes on every login attempt.

author: Tina Sitwell

insight: User experienced a frustrating update loop where the app repeatedly prompted for updates despite already completing the installation.

You’re Unit price is too High

author: Chris
body: App is not working

author: Chris Bingham
body: Spoke to a lady in south Africa very helpful till we got cut off. Conversation with a gent telling me to turn of my smart meter as it is not showing my gas, he has told me to turn it off for 72 hours don't have a lot of confidence.
insight: Customer received unhelpful and potentially incorrect advice about turning off their smart meter, compounded by a dropped call with offshore support.

author: Susan Owen

author: Hzy
body: Scottish power are not the cheapest and have billed me an extortionate bill due to claiming money back thats my money anyway . Scottish power have on neumerous accounts put me in debt
insight: Customer is dissatisfied with high billing charges and repeated debt issues caused by disputed money collection practices.

author: Mrs Ferguson
body: Got a text wanting me to renew my payment details but also a request for a meter reading because you couldn't get a reading and if I didn't send a reading my gas and electric could be cut when I checked both gas and electricity readings were both showing on the app only different by about a days use
insight: Customer frustrated by payment renewal request and meter reading demand despite readings being available on the app with only minor discrepancies.

author: Ray MILLER
body: Using the link provided in my email notification, and filling in the appropriate details, it then told me it couldn't use my pre registered credit card. I had to exit, log into my account and try again. It then accepted my pre registered card with no problem. This happens every time.
insight: Payment processing fails intermittently when using email notification links, requiring users to manually log in and retry to complete transactions.

author: Karen Smith
body: Happy customer
insight: Customer expresses overall satisfaction with ScottishPower's service.

author: Martin Weissman
body: Great features, easy to use!
insight: User praises the app's user-friendly interface and feature set.

author: Paul Daley
body: very easy to navigate around and fast response..
insight: User praises the app's intuitive interface and quick customer support response times.

author: Heather Nicholson
body: you have changed your website and i have been unable to access my account , i eventually  called and my enquiry was resolved, why change your website when it is not fit for purposes  the adviser steve was very helpful
insight: Website redesign created account access problems, though customer service agent Steve ultimately resolved the issue.

author: Mike
body: Same answer I had 1 year ago

author: john Pile
body: You sent an engineer out to change the smart meters he did not do it i got the impression he did nt want to do it, I waited for 6weeks and now have to rebook an engineer again
insight: Engineer failed to install smart meters and customer must reschedule after a 6-week wait.

author: JM
body: Had to wait a good few weeks to be able to use app after I registered. Not happy that I was quoted a monthly amount to then be told we needed to increase our monthly payments by over £28 . Plus saying we would be over £300 in debt at the end of year. We have just transferred to Scottish power . First payment just been taken and we are already £60 in credit
insight: Customer frustrated by inaccurate billing estimates and forced payment increases, though noting better credit position after switching to ScottishPower.

author: Allison
body: In 2025 my credit balance was good.  Scottish Power advised me to reduce the monthly payments.  This time I went along with it.   Now I am in deficit and have to increase my monthly payments.  Won't do this again.  I felt I was in control before and now I am not and do not like to owe anything.
insight: Customer regrets following Scottish Power's advice to reduce monthly payments and now faces a deficit, feeling loss of control over their account management.

author: Michelle  McCann
body:
After 2 attempts at no shows for installation of Smart meter 28/04 and 10/07/2025 i was  only credited  with 1 compensation payment having been told I would receive for each missed appointment and having stayed in all day  I had to make contact to both Sureserve and Scot Power to find out what had happened
I do wish to add that the hand display unit has never worked I was quickly handed it and was told it would display maybe overnight and was not physically shown how the meter worked
Poor show for a company I have been with for over 30 years
insight: Customer experienced multiple missed smart meter installations and received incomplete compensation despite staying in all day, plus inadequate training on the device.

author: Graham Hill

author: amanda

author: Mrs H B
body: I wanted to set up a direct debit. I clicked on "review tarriff & set up direct debit" and was taken to info on different tarriffs. I could not find anywhere to set up the direct debit. I still have not managed to set up the direct debit.

author: Jammlry
body: Electric meter changed but still have to send in readings. Gas meter still old one & still have to read meter ?
insight: User is frustrated that despite having their electric meter upgraded, they still need to manually submit readings instead of having a fully automated smart meter system.

author: AIVIN BIJU
body: Scottish Power is a decent energy provider overall, but some of their processes can be a bit tricky and the waiting times can sometimes be quite long. That said, the staff are generally very helpful and do their best to resolve any issues once you get through to them. When you are able to speak with someone, they are usually friendly and willing to explain things clearly. While the system could be more efficient, the support from the staff makes the experience better.
insight: Staff helpfulness is undermined by inefficient processes and long wait times to reach support.

author: James Towers
body: I can't log in to power saver app
insight: User is unable to access the power saver app due to a login failure.

author: Pete Green
body: Changing g my tariff I'd give 5 stars.     My IHD not worked since 2019 which i reported on 04/10/2019.And numerous since. Still not working and told there is a  shortage and I could buy one if I wished. So in over 5 years they have not had 1 in stock which I could have had?? Shocking.
insight: User is satisfied with tariff changes but extremely frustrated that their faulty In-Home Display device has remained unrepaired for over 5 years despite multiple reports due to claimed stock shortages.

author: Jane Whitehead
body: Stuck in a circle of updating and opening the app

insight: User appreciates the app's functionality for managing payments and discovering cost-saving opportunities.

author: Roger Mills

author: Simon

insight: User appreciates the app's intuitive interface and comprehensive feature set for managing their account and billing information.

author: Jan Hennig
body: No details on cost of power. Only a sum to pay monthly and opportunity to commit to the new tariff, but no indication of the costs per unit. Next day change of supply instead of at the end of the current term. So disgruntled.
insight: User is frustrated by lack of transparency on per-unit energy costs and an unexpected immediate supply change instead of waiting until their current term ends.

author: Alexander Moran
body: easy to use. rewards are great
insight: User appreciates the app's intuitive interface and finds the rewards programme valuable.

author: DEBRA HIXSON
body: See below for answer
insight: Review provides insufficient information to assess specific issues with the app or service.

author: John Marriott
body: not user friendly
insight: The app's user interface and design are difficult to navigate and understand.

author: Audrey Dodds
body: A bit frustrating as initially representative didn't exactly understand what I wanted answering.  We got there in the end but only to give me another number to call.
insight: Customer service representative failed to understand the initial query and ultimately redirected the caller rather than resolving the issue.

author: Leanne
body:
I have had an account with scottish power for 5 years and I needed to remove someones name off the account. The only way to do this apparently was to delete my account and restart, I specifically asked if this would affect my payments because I had managed to get them down to a decent amount and was told no - unsurprisingly when the new account was set up the amounts had changed and risen by £27 a month. I believe this was because I was in credit over £200 with my previous account which of course wouldnt be refunded to me (in case it was needed) until they closed the account and were forced to give the money back. I offered to make an upfront payment but was told it wouldnt bring my payments down to anywhere near the previous amount. 

Very frustrating and unhelpful and will now impact me financially.
insight: Customer's monthly payments increased significantly after being forced to close and restart their account to remove a name, with credit balance withheld until final closure.

author: Brian
body:
Impossible to contact Scot Pwr by 'phone - tried repeatedly - GAVE UP.
Did it all on-line - reluctantly - I WANTED TO SPEAK TO SOMEONE!
insight: Customer is frustrated by inability to reach phone support and was forced to use the app as an unwanted alternative.

author: Graham Bryce
body:
I didn't like the fact that you changed your sign in requirements without telling those who use your app and internet account what they were and I didn't like your 'chat' function which is no more than an unmanned decision tree that failed utterly to include my problem.

I did like your agent on the phone and the speed with which I got through.  I also liked the fact that she identified my problem and succesfully fixed it. 

In short, your frontline people on the phone are very good.  Your backroom  IT geeks are thoughtless and clearly never think of their ultimate customers when making changes that affect them. This leads to wasted time and frayed nerves.

To be fair, backroom IT geeks tend to behave this way whomever they work for, leaving the poor old phone agent to sort out the mess.
insight: App authentication changes were implemented without user notification and the chat support function is inadequate, though phone support staff are highly effective.

author: Tara-yogini
body: This app repeatedly refuses my meter readings and I have to go on live chat to submit them. Kind of defeats the purpose of having an app. Since re loading the app (on advice from agent) it now wont accept my login details till I try to open a new account. Then it logs in. It requires an authentication to log in to the account now but the emails sometimes take more than the 5 mins they allow before they come in. Very frustrating.  Is easier to go online and login to the account without the app. Its a complete waste of time on my iphone.
insight: Multiple app functionality failures including meter reading submission errors and authentication issues make the app unreliable compared to the web platform.

author: P and mrs c PATON
body: Why pester your customers to put smart meters in there property when you know that they won’t work because the signal is not good
insight: Customer frustrated with company pushing smart meter installation despite known signal coverage issues in their area.

author: S!@
body: I find the app incredibly easy to operate. I just inserted the meter reading and received the price for the units I had used immediately. Fantastic app very handy and easy to use 👍
insight: User praises the app's intuitive design and quick meter reading submission process with immediate cost calculation.

body: I don’t agree with the high standing charges in energy rich Scotland

author: James Rutherford
body:
The system is easy  to review and update tariffs but not easy to make comparisons. I ended up paying a little more than expected for evening rates. Still we will see how it goes if there are any increases due to the present conflict.
Additional: Got a problem now. My biometric sign on has gone and there is no option to bring it back. All that just for changing tariff? Seems wrong to me.
insight: User finds tariff comparison and updates cumbersome, paid more than expected, and lost biometric login functionality with no recovery option.

author: Caroline Dear
body: Ran up debt because they wouldn’t send an electric bill when we were first transferred despite contacting them often. Smart meters have never worked. Always had to read the meters. As soon as we are clear we will go elsewhere
insight: Customer accumulated debt due to missing bills and non-functional smart meters, combined with poor responsiveness to contact attempts, driving them to switch providers.

author: Chris
body: totally horrible .never use this app or company. had issues from the very start they don't care about there customers just want money . any issues or complaints you will get an initial response and then never replied to after that

author: Mike Binks

author: Beverley Frost
